<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">break-up</FONT></I>. Pursuant to the memorandum of understanding with Ericsson, which described the principles for the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">break-up</FONT> and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">wind-down</FONT>
of the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> joint venture, <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> activities were split into three&nbsp;main parts. The first part included the design, development and related expenses
associated with the R&amp;D costs for the LTE multimode thin modem products, which since March&nbsp;2, 2013, has been fully funded by Ericsson. The second part related to the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> business on existing
products, French and Italian employees and the Calamba and Muar facilities, which have been, since March&nbsp;2, 2013, fully accounted for in our accounts with no attribution to noncontrolling interest in our Consolidated Statement of Income. The
third&nbsp;part was related to the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">wind-down</FONT> activities remaining at <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson,</FONT> equally funded by both parties. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On July&nbsp;17, 2013, we and Ericsson signed a Definitive Framework Agreement in line with the principles described
above, which established the split of <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson&#146;s</FONT> activities between ST and Ericsson, the form of the transfers, the allocation of intangibles, the allocation of intellectual property, the assumption of
liabilities, the principle of a balanced <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">break-up</FONT> applied through equal cash funding at closing and the tail payment by Ericsson to ST in case of an onward sale by Ericsson of the LTE multimode thin modem
business. In addition, we and Ericsson also signed funding commitment letters to the residual joint <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">wind-down</FONT> operations to ensure solvency capped at $149&nbsp;million for each partner. Additionally, Ericsson
and ST have released an indemnity letter, on terms that are customary under such circumstances, to ST-Ericsson&#146;s directors and officers, which include the CEO, the CFO and other senior executives of our company. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On August&nbsp;2, 2013, the transaction closed and various activities were transferred to the respective parents,
including the sale of our shares in <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> JVD, and the sale by <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> JVS of its Swedish subsidiary, in each case, to Ericsson. At the same time, certain
businesses have been sold by ST-Ericsson to third parties. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">At the beginning of September&nbsp;2013, we sold
one share of <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> JVS to Ericsson for its nominal value and signed the new shareholder agreement, changing the ownership structure of <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> JVS to bring
both partners to an equal ownership proportion. As a consequence, we determined that while we no longer have control over the entity, we retain a significant influence and account for it under the equity method. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As a result of the foregoing, the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT>
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">break-up</FONT> was completed during the third quarter of 2013 and ST-Ericsson results have been deconsolidated since September&nbsp;1, 2013. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Share-based</FONT> compensation</I>. We measure the cost of <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">share-based</FONT> service awards based on the fair value of the award on the grant date. In 2013, our <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">share-based</FONT> compensation plans awarded shares contingent on the achievement of
certain performance conditions based on financial objectives, including our financial results when compared to certain industry performances. In 2013, approximately <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">one-half</FONT> of the shares awarded were
contingent on the achievement of certain performance conditions. In order to determine <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">share-based</FONT> compensation to be recorded for the period, we use significant estimates on the number of awards expected to
vest, including the probability of achieving the fixed performance conditions including those relating to industry performances compared to our financial results, and our best estimates of award forfeitures and employees&#146; service periods. Our
assumptions related to industry performance are generally taken with a one&nbsp;quarter lag in line with the availability of market information. In 2013, we recorded a total charge of approximately $26&nbsp;million relating to our outstanding stock
award plans. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Income </I>(<I>loss</I>)<I> on <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Equity-method</FONT>
Investments</I>. We record our share in the results of entities that we account for under the equity method. This recognition is based on results reported by these entities, relying on their internal reporting systems to measure financial results.
In case of triggering events, such as continuing difficult market conditions, which could lead to continued operating losses and negative cash flow, or in the case of a strategic repositioning by one or more of our partners, we determine whether our
investment is temporarily or other-than-temporarily impaired. If impairment is considered to be other-than-temporary, we need to assess the fair value of our investment and record an impairment charge directly in earnings when fair value is lower
than the carrying value of the investment. We make this assessment by evaluating the business on the basis of the most recent plans and projections or to the best of our estimates. In 2013, we recognized a loss of approximately $104&nbsp;million
related to our equity investment in 3Sun largely due to a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-cash</FONT> charge of $69&nbsp;million on our equity value in 3Sun as share of losses in the investment due to impairment charges reported by 3Sun. As of
December&nbsp;31, 2013, we reported $30 million assets related to the combination of equity investment and parent loan in 3Sun. In addition, we recognized a loss of $18&nbsp;million related to other investments, including our share of losses in <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> JVS for about $7&nbsp;million which has been accounted for under the equity method since September&nbsp;1, 2013 and a loss of approximately $6&nbsp;million related to our equity investment in MicroOLED
SAS, primarily corresponding to the impairment of our remaining investment. We are continuing to monitor our equity </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">44 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">
investments and, if required, additional <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">other-than-temporary</FONT></FONT> impairment charges could negatively impact our future
results. As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, the value in our Consolidated Balance Sheets of our equity investments was $63&nbsp;million. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Financial assets</I>. We classify our financial assets in the following two&nbsp;categories, trading and
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">available-for-sale.</FONT></FONT> Such classification depends on the purpose for which the investments are acquired. We determine the classification of our financial assets at initial
recognition. Unlisted equity securities with no readily determinable fair value are carried at cost; they are neither classified as trading nor as <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">available-for-sale</FONT></FONT>
financial assets. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Trading and
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">available-for-sale</FONT></FONT> financial assets are measured at fair value. The fair value of quoted debt and equity securities is based on current market prices. If the market for
a financial asset is not active, if no observable market price is obtainable, or if the security is not quoted, we measure fair value by using assumptions and estimates. For unquoted equity securities, these assumptions and estimates include the use
of recent <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">arm&#146;s-length</FONT> transactions; for debt securities without available observable market price, we establish fair value by reference to publicly available indexes of securities with the same rating
and comparable or similar underlying collaterals or industries&#146; exposure, which we believe approximates the amount that would be received from the sale of the asset in an orderly transaction between market participants. In measuring fair value,
we make maximum use of market inputs and minimize the use of unobservable inputs. As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, the value in our Consolidated Balance Sheet of our financial assets was $57&nbsp;million invested in senior debt floating rate notes
classified as assets <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">available-for-sale.</FONT></FONT> </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Income taxes</I>. We make estimates and judgments in determining income tax for the period, comprising current and deferred income tax. We need to assess the income tax expected to be paid or the tax
benefit expected to be received related to the current&nbsp;year taxable profit and loss in each individual tax jurisdiction and recognize deferred income tax for all temporary differences arising between the tax bases of assets and liabilities and
their carrying amount in the Consolidated Financial Statements. Furthermore, we assess all material open income tax positions in all tax jurisdictions to determine any uncertain tax positions, and to record a provision for those that are not more
likely than not to be sustained upon examination by the taxing authorities, which could require potential tax claims or assessments in various jurisdictions. In such an event and in case any tax assessment exceeds our provisions, we could be
required to record additional charges in our accounts, which could significantly exceed our best estimates and our existing provisions. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We also assess the likelihood of realization of our deferred tax assets originated by our net operating loss carry forwards. The ultimate realization of deferred tax assets is dependent upon, among other
things, our ability to generate future taxable profit available against loss carry forwards or tax credits before their expiration or our ability to implement prudent and feasible tax planning strategies or the possibility to settle uncertain tax
positions against available net operating loss carry forwards or similar tax losses and credits. We record a valuation allowance against the deferred tax assets when we consider it is more likely than not that the deferred tax assets will not be
realized. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, we had current deferred tax assets of $123&nbsp;million and <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-current</FONT> deferred tax assets of $227&nbsp;million, net of valuation allowances. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We could be required to record further valuation allowances thereby reducing the amount of total deferred tax assets, resulting in an increase of our income tax charge, if our estimates of projected
future taxable income and benefits from available tax strategies are reduced as a result of a change in our assessment or due to other factors, or if changes in current tax regulations are enacted that impose restrictions on the timing or extent of
our ability to utilize net operating losses and tax credit <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">carry-forwards</FONT> in the future. Likewise, a change in the tax rates applicable in the various jurisdictions or unfavorable outcomes of any ongoing tax
audits could have a material impact on our future tax provisions in the periods in which these changes could occur. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Patent and other Intellectual Property </I>(&#147;<I>IP</I>&#148;)<I> litigation or claims</I>. As is the case with many companies in the semiconductor industry, we have from time to time received, and
may in the future receive, communications alleging possible infringement of patents and other IP rights of third parties. Furthermore, we may become involved in costly litigation brought against us regarding patents, mask works, copyrights,
trademarks or trade secrets. In the event the outcome of a litigation claim is unfavorable to us, we may be required to take a license for the underlying IP right on economically unfavorable terms and conditions, possibly pay damages for prior use,
and/or face an injunction, all of which singly or in the aggregate could have a material adverse effect on our results of operations and on our ability to compete. See &#147;Item&nbsp;3. Key Information &#151; Risk Factors &#151; Risks Related to
Our Operations &#151; We depend on patents to protect our rights to our technology and may face claims of infringing the IP rights of others&#148;. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">45 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We record a provision when we believe that it is probable that a liability
has been incurred and the amount of the loss can be reasonably estimated. We regularly evaluate losses and claims to determine whether they need to be adjusted based on current information available to us. Such estimates are difficult to the extent
that they are largely dependent on the status of ongoing litigation that may vary based on positions taken by the Court with respect to issues submitted, demands of opposing parties, changing laws, discovery of new facts or other matters of fact or
law. As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, based on our current evaluation of ongoing litigation and claims we face, we have not estimated any amounts that could have a material impact on our results of operations and financial condition with respect to
probable risks. We currently estimate that possible losses for known claims are in the range of $30&nbsp;million to $50&nbsp;million. In the event of litigation that is adversely determined with respect to our interests, or in the event that we need
to change our evaluation of a potential <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">third-party</FONT> claim based on new evidence, facts or communications, unexpected rulings or changes in the law, this could have a material adverse effect on our results of
operations or financial condition at the time it were to materialize. We are in discussion with several parties with respect to claims against us relating to possible infringement of IP rights. We are also involved in certain legal proceedings
concerning such issues. See &#147;Item&nbsp;8. Financial Information &#151; Legal Proceedings&#148;. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Other
claims</I>. We are subject to the possibility of loss contingencies arising in the ordinary course of business. These include, but are not limited to: warranty costs on our products not covered by insurance, breach of contract claims, tax claims
beyond assessed uncertain tax positions as well as claims for environmental damages. We are also exposed to numerous legal risks which until now have not resulted in legal disputes and proceedings. These include risks related to product recalls,
environment, <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">anti-trust,</FONT> <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">anti-corruption</FONT> and competition as well as other compliance regulations. We may also face claims in the event of breaches of law committed by
individual employees or third parties. In determining loss contingencies, we consider the likelihood of a loss of an asset or the occurrence of a liability, as well as our ability to reasonably estimate the amount of such loss or liability. An
estimated loss is recorded when we believe that it is probable that a liability has been incurred and the amount of the loss can be reasonably estimated. We regularly <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">re-evaluate</FONT> any losses and claims and
determine whether our provisions need to be adjusted based on the current information available to us. As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, based on our current evaluation of ongoing litigation and claims we face, we have not estimated any amounts that
could have a material impact on our results of operations and financial condition with respect to either probable or possible risks. In the event we are unable to accurately estimate the amount of such loss in a correct and timely manner, this could
have a material adverse effect on our results of operations or financial condition at the time such loss was to materialize. For further details of our legal proceedings refer to &#147;Item&nbsp;8. Financial Information &#151; Legal
Proceedings&#148; and Note 22 to our Consolidated Financial Statements. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">There can be no assurance that all IP
litigation or claims and other claims to which we are currently subject will be resolved in our favor or as currently anticipated. If the outcome of any claim or litigation were to be unfavorable to us, we could incur monetary damages, and/or face
an injunction, all of which singly or in the aggregate could have an adverse effect on our results of operations and our ability to compete. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Pension and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Post-Employment</FONT> Benefits</I>. Our results of operations and our Consolidated Balance Sheets include amounts for pension obligations and <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">post-employment</FONT> benefits that are measured using actuarial valuations. At December&nbsp;31, 2013, our pension and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">post-employment</FONT> benefit obligations net of plan assets
amounted to $366&nbsp;million. These valuations are based on key assumptions, including discount rates, expected <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">long-term</FONT> rates of return on funds, turnover rates and salary increase rates. These assumptions
used in the determination of the net periodic benefit cost are updated on an annual basis at the beginning of each fiscal year or more frequently upon the occurrence of significant events. Any changes in the pension schemes or in the above
assumptions can have an impact on our valuations. The measurement date we use for our plans is December&nbsp;31. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As a consequence of our decision to downsize our UK operations, we have proposed that the UK pension schemes (the Bristol Scheme and the Marlow Scheme) be merged, which will generate moderate funding
savings and provide the Trustees with additional security through a larger and stronger principal employer. The merger of the two schemes is still under discussion with the Trustees and is not expected to materially change our pension liabilities.
</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>Fiscal Year 2013 </I></B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Under Article&nbsp;35 of our Articles of Association, our financial year extends from January&nbsp;1 to December&nbsp;31, which is the period end of each fiscal year. The first quarter of 2013 ended on
March&nbsp;30, 2013. The second quarter of 2013 ended on June&nbsp;29, 2013 and the third quarter of 2013 ended on September&nbsp;28, 2013. The fourth </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">46 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">
quarter of 2013 ended on December&nbsp;31, 2013. Based on our fiscal calendar, the distribution of our revenues and expenses by quarter may be unbalanced due to a different number of days in the
various quarters of the fiscal year. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In 2014 the first quarter will end on March&nbsp;29, the second quarter
will end on June&nbsp;28, the third quarter will end on September&nbsp;27 and the fourth quarter will end on December&nbsp;31. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>2013 Business Overview </I></B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The total available market is defined as the &#147;TAM&#148;, while the serviceable available market, the &#147;SAM&#148;, is defined as the market for products produced by us (which consists of the TAM
and excludes major devices such as Microprocessors (&#147;MPUs&#148;), DRAMs, optoelectronics devices and Flash Memories and, as a consequence of our exit from <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> activities, excludes also the
Wireless Application Specific market (Broadband and Application Processor)). </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Based on published industry data
by WSTS, semiconductor industry revenues increased in 2013 on a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">year-over-year</FONT></FONT> basis by approximately 5% for the TAM to reach about $306&nbsp;billion. The SAM declined
by approximately 2% to reach about $139&nbsp;billion. In the fourth quarter, the TAM and the SAM increased on a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">year-over-year</FONT></FONT> basis by approximately 8% and 3%,
respectively. Sequentially, in the fourth quarter of 2013, the TAM and the SAM decreased by approximately 1% and 2%, respectively. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">With reference to our business performance, in 2013, we registered a decline of 4.8% in terms of revenues as a consequence of our exit from <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson.</FONT> Excluding
the Wireless product line, our revenues increased 3.2%, a better performance than the SAM, with the main contributions coming from our microcontrollers and automotive products. We also made good progress on our customer diversification, mass market
and distribution initiatives, with no customers above 10% and an increase of revenues in distribution, which was up by approximately 3 percentage points, reaching a 26% share of total revenues. Our fourth quarter 2013 revenues amounted to
$2,015&nbsp;million, a 0.1% increase on a sequential basis, within our guidance (flat, plus or minus 3.5&nbsp;percentage points). The sequential increase in our revenues is the result of an increase of 2.3% in our Sense&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Power and
Automotive Products (SP&amp;A) segment, while our Embedded Processing Solutions (EPS) segment decreased by 3.2%, mainly due to declining revenues in IBP product line by 11.7%. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">year-over</FONT> year basis, our revenues decreased by 6.8%, which is the result of
an increase of 4.2%&nbsp;in our SP&amp;A segment whereas our EPS segment decreased 19.4%, mainly driven by the phasing out of the Wireless product line and weak demand for <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">set-top</FONT> box legacy products in the
Digital Convergence product line. Excluding the Wireless product line, on a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">year-over-year</FONT></FONT> basis, our revenues in the fourth quarter of 2013 increased by 3.9% and EPS
revenues by 5.0%. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our effective average exchange rate for 2013 and 2012 was $1.31 for &#128;1.00. Our
effective average exchange rate for the fourth quarter of 2013 was $1.34 for &#128;1.00, compared to $1.31 for &#128;1.00 for the third&nbsp;quarter of 2013 and $1.30 for &#128;1.00 in the fourth quarter of 2012. For a more detailed discussion of
our hedging arrangements and the impact of fluctuations in exchange rates, see &#147;Impact of Changes in Exchange Rates&#148; below. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our 2013 gross margin was 32.3% of revenues, decreasing by 50 basis points compared to the prior year, primarily due to the negative impact of selling prices and lower technology licensing revenues,
partially offset by improved manufacturing efficiencies, lower unused capacity charges and the absence of the $53&nbsp;million <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">one-time</FONT> charge related to the 2012 arbitration award to NXP. Our fourth quarter
2013 gross margin was 32.9%, increasing by 50 basis points on a sequential basis, reflecting improved manufacturing efficiencies, partially offset by a negative currency effect, the negative impact of selling prices and a higher amount of unused
capacity charges. Our fourth quarter 2013 gross margin was within our guidance (33% plus or minus 2 percentage points). </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In 2013, we made solid progress in executing the strategy we announced in December&nbsp;2012 but we still have much to accomplish. We completed the split up of
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> in a timely manner and by adding some of their competencies, we strengthened our product development teams. Our combined selling, general and administrative (SG&amp;A) and research and development
(R&amp;D) expenses amounted to $2,882&nbsp;million, a significant decrease compared to $3,579&nbsp;million in the prior year, primarily due to the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">wind-down</FONT>
and the initial benefits of our ongoing restructuring initiatives. Furthermore, we brought our fourth&nbsp;quarter 2013&nbsp;operating expenses down by about 25% compared to the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">year-ago</FONT> quarter and our fourth
quarter net operating expenses (combined SG&amp;A and R&amp;D expenses net of R&amp;D grants) within our target range of $600&nbsp;million to $650&nbsp;million per quarter. We also started to make gradual structural changes to our manufacturing
footprint which will benefit our gross margin and we announced a key frame agreement with the French government to support our R&amp;D efforts for CMOS derivative technology. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">47 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Impairment and restructuring charges significantly decreased to
$292&nbsp;million from $1,376&nbsp;million in 2012 mainly due to lower impairment charges. 2012 included a $1,234&nbsp;million <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-cash</FONT> impairment charge on Wireless goodwill and other intangible assets.
</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our operating losses were $465&nbsp;million in 2013, improving compared to the loss of $2,081&nbsp;million in
2012. The improvement in our operating losses in 2013 was mainly driven by our reduction of operating expenses and lower impairment charges. By product segment, the main improvement was registered by EPS which benefited from our exit of <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson.</FONT> Fourth quarter operating result before impairment and restructuring charges was an $18&nbsp;million operating income improving from an operating loss of $142&nbsp;million in the prior year quarter,
mainly due to a reduction in operating expenses primarily due to our exit of <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> and the benefits of our ongoing restructuring initiatives. Furthermore, our free cash flow was positive in the
fourth&nbsp;quarter of 2013. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>Business Outlook </I></B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In the first quarter, we expect overall revenues to decrease sequentially by about 9.5% at the midpoint plus or minus 3.5%
percentage points. First quarter revenues reflect, on top of seasonality including the New Year holiday in Asia, a drop in revenues from <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> legacy products of more than half from the fourth quarter of
2013 level. As a result, gross margin in the first quarter is expected to be about 32.4%, plus or minus 2.0&nbsp;percentage points. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">While the semiconductor market did not perform as expected in 2013, we are encouraged by the positive <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">macro-economic</FONT> signs and by the market dynamics expected in
2014. We are well positioned to capture opportunities and to continue to grow faster than the market we serve as we focus on product leadership in Sense&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Power and Automotive and in Embedded Processing. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In 2014, we plan to advance towards our operating margin target of about 10%, expected by
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">mid-2015,</FONT> based on a combination of revenue growth, gross margin improvement and reduction of net expenses towards the low end of our target range. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We expect the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Nano-2017</FONT> R&amp;D grants to become effective in the first quarter
of 2014, subject to the approval by the European Union. There is no guarantee that the program will be approved or if it is approved, that there will be no modifications that could negatively affect the R&amp;D program, all of which could have a
material adverse effect on our results of operations. See &#147;Item&nbsp;3. Key Information &#151; Risk Factors &#151; Risks Related to Our Operations &#151; If we fail to receive the necessary funding for our R&amp;D program, we may have to
reconsider our strategy, which could adversely impact our results of operations.&#148; </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>This outlook is
based on an assumed effective currency exchange rate of approximately $1</I>.<I>35 to &#128;1</I>.<I>00</I>&nbsp;<I>for the 2014 first quarter and includes the impact of existing hedging contracts</I>.<I> The first quarter will close on
March&nbsp;29, 2014</I>.<I> </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>These are <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">forward-looking</FONT>
statements that are subject to known and unknown risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially</I>;<I> in particular, refer to those known risks and uncertainties described in </I>&#147;<I>Cautionary Note Regarding <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Forward-Looking</FONT> Statements</I>&#148;<I> and </I>&#147;<I>Item&nbsp;3</I>.<I> Key Information &#151; Risk Factors</I>&#148;<I> herein</I>.<I> </I></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>Other Developments </I></B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On March&nbsp;11, 2013, we <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">re-asserted</FONT> our MEMS technology and patent leadership with the filing, by our U.S. subsidiary, STMicroelectronics, Inc., of a complaint
with the United States International Trade Commission (ITC) requesting an investigation into the alleged infringement of five ST patents covering all of InvenSense, Inc.&#146;s MEMS device offerings, as well as products from two&nbsp;of
InvenSense&#146;s customers, Black and Decker, Inc. and Roku, Inc. As part of the filing, we requested that the ITC issue an order excluding InvenSense&#146;s infringing gyroscopes and accelerometers, as well its customers&#146; products that
include those InvenSense devices, from importation into the United States. On February&nbsp;10, 2014, we announced that we have settled all pending proceedings between us and InvenSense and have entered into a patent cross license agreement. Under
the terms of the settlement, InvenSense made a one-time $15 million payment in the first quarter of 2014 but neither we nor InvenSense has made any admission of liability. We will collect royalties under the terms of the patent cross license in the
future. The expected royalties will not be material to our financial results. Other terms between the parties are confidential. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On May&nbsp;28, 2013, we announced that ST-Ericsson sold the assets and intellectual property rights associated with its mobile connectivity Global Navigation Satellite System business to a leading
semiconductor company. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">48 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">
In addition to the assets and intellectual property rights associated with this business, a world class team of 130 industry veterans located in Daventry (UK), Bangalore (India) and Singapore
joined the buyer at closing of the transaction which occurred in August 2013. A gain of $66 million has been registered in our consolidated financial statements. On August&nbsp;5, 2013, we and Ericsson announced the closing of the split up of
ST-Ericsson, less than nine months after we announced our strategic plan. We have taken on some of the existing ST-Ericsson products as well as certain assembly and test facilities. In total, approximately 1,000 employees have joined
STMicroelectronics. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On March&nbsp;17, 2013, we repaid with available cash the residual outstanding 2013
Senior Bonds. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On March&nbsp;26, 2013, we signed a new Euro 350&nbsp;million loan agreement with the European
Investment Bank (&#147;EIB&#148;). The facility, with final maturity eight&nbsp;years after disbursement, was fully drawn by us in the fourth&nbsp;quarter of 2013 in U.S. dollars. This new facility supports our activities in R&amp;D and innovation
related to the design and realization of the next generation of technologies and electronic devices. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On
May&nbsp;21, 2013, we announced our leadership of Places2Be, a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">3-year,</FONT> &#128;360&nbsp;million <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">advanced-technology</FONT> <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">pilot-line</FONT>
project with the participation of 18 other leading European companies and academic institutions to support the industrialization of <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Fully-Depleted</FONT> <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Silicon-On-Insulator</FONT></FONT> <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">(FD-SOI)</FONT> microelectronics technology. Places2Be (&#147;Pilot Lines for Advanced CMOS Enhanced by SOI in 2x nodes, Built in Europe&#148;) aims to
support the deployment of an <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">FD-SOI</FONT> pilot line at <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">28-nm</FONT> and the subsequent node, as well as a dual source that will enable volume manufacturing in Europe. Places2Be will
drive the creation of a European microelectronics design ecosystem using this <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">FD-SOI</FONT> platform and explore the path towards the next step for this technology (14/10-nm). </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On June&nbsp;17, 2013, we announced that we had signed a comprehensive agreement with Rambus Inc. expanding existing
licenses between the two&nbsp;companies, settling all outstanding claims, and committing both organizations to explore additional opportunities for collaboration. The multifaceted agreement gives Rambus access to our
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Fully-Depleted</FONT> Silicon On Insulator <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">(FD-SOI)</FONT> <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">process-technology</FONT> design environment while giving us secured license terms from the
Cryptography Research, Inc. (CRI) division of Rambus that makes it possible for us to expand deployment of security technology for banking, identity, PayTV, video gaming, smartphones, and government, across a wider range of products. </FONT></P>

<TD WIDTH="5%"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="justify"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">&#149;</FONT></P></TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Authorization to our Managing Board, for eighteen&nbsp;months as of our 2013 Annual General Meeting, to repurchase our shares, subject to the
approval of our Supervisory Board. </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On July&nbsp;22, 2013, we announced the <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Nano-2017</FONT> Research and Development program, a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">five-year</FONT> <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">public-private</FONT> strategic R&amp;D program led by us to further advance our
leadership in key embedded processing solutions and technologies. The project draws support from a broad coalition of French national, regional and local authorities as well as by the European community through the ENIAC Joint Technology Initiative.
Funding for the program is subject to approval by the European Commission. Ultimately, <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Nano-2017&nbsp;strengthens</FONT> our leadership in such key technologies as <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">FD-SOI</FONT> <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">(low-power,</FONT> <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">high-performance</FONT> processing), <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">next-generation</FONT> imaging (sensors and image signal processors), and <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">next-generation</FONT> embedded <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-volatile</FONT> memories. These technologies are at the core of our embedded processing solutions which include microcontrollers, imaging solutions,
digital consumer products, application processors and digital ASICs. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">49 </FONT></P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On July&nbsp;24, 2013, we announced the publication of our 2012
Sustainability Report. Our sixteenth annual Sustainability Report contains comprehensive details of our Sustainability strategy, policies and performance during 2012. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On December&nbsp;2, 2013, we announced that our Shareholders had adopted all resolutions proposed at the Extraordinary
General Meeting (&#147;EGM&#148;) held on December&nbsp;2, 2013: </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

</FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

the distribution of quarterly dividends from the reserves of the Company. Immediately after the EGM was held, the Articles of Association of the Company were amended by means of the execution of a notarial deed of amendment.
</FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>Results of Operations </I></B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>Segment Information </I></B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We operate in two&nbsp;business areas: Semiconductors and Subsystems. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In the Semiconductors business area, we design, develop, manufacture and market a broad range of products, including discrete and standard commodity components,
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">application-specific</FONT> integrated circuits (&#147;ASICs&#148;), <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">full-custom</FONT> devices and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">semi-custom</FONT> devices and <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">application-specific</FONT> standard products (&#147;ASSPs&#148;) for analog, digital and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">mixed-signal</FONT> applications. In addition, we further participate in the manufacturing value
chain of Smartcard products, which include the production and sale of both silicon chips and Smartcards. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Effective January&nbsp;1, 2013, our segment reporting reflects our strategy announced on December&nbsp;10, 2012. Our
strategy takes into account the evolution of the markets we are in and the environment we see in the years to come and is based on our leadership in our two&nbsp;product segments, supported by a Sales&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Marketing organization with a
particular focus on our major accounts, as well as expanding our penetration of the mass market and focusing on five growth drivers: Automotive Products, Application Processors, including Digital Consumer Products, MEMS and Sensors, Microcontrollers
and Smart Power. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our segments are as follows: </FONT></P>

STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In 2013, we revised our results from prior periods in accordance with the new segment structure. The preparation of segment information based on the current segment structure requires us to make estimates
and assumptions in determining the operating income (loss) of the segments for the prior reporting periods. We believe that the revised 2012 and 2011 presentation is consistent with that of 2013 and we use these comparatives when managing our
company. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In the Subsystems business area, we design, develop, manufacture and market subsystems and modules
for the telecommunications, automotive and industrial markets including mobile phone accessories, battery chargers, ISDN power supplies and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">in-vehicle</FONT> equipment for electronic toll payment. Based on its
immateriality to our business as a whole, the Subsystems business area does not meet the requirements for a reportable segment as defined in the guidance on disclosures about segments of an enterprise and related information. All the financial
values related to Subsystems including net revenues and related costs, are reported in the segment &#147;Others&#148;. </FONT></P>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The following tables present our consolidated net revenues and consolidated
operating income (loss) by product segment. For the computation of the segments&#146; internal financial measurements, we use certain internal rules of allocation for the costs not directly chargeable to the segments, including cost of sales,
selling, general and administrative (&#147;SG&amp;A&#148;) expenses and a part of research and development (&#147;R&amp;D&#148;) expenses. In compliance with our internal policies, certain cost items are not charged to the segments, including
impairment, restructuring charges and other related closure costs, including <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> plans, unused capacity charges, <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">phase-out</FONT> and
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">start-up</FONT> costs of certain manufacturing facilities, certain <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">one-time</FONT> corporate items such as the 2012 NXP arbitration award charge, strategic and special R&amp;D
programs or other <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">corporate-sponsored</FONT> initiatives, including certain <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">corporate-level</FONT> operating expenses and certain other miscellaneous charges. In addition,
depreciation and amortization expense is part of the manufacturing costs allocated to the product segments and is neither identified as part of the inventory variation nor as part of the unused capacity charges; therefore, it cannot be isolated in
the costs of goods sold. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:12px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The following table sets forth certain financial data from our Consolidated

<TR BGCOLOR="#cceeff">
<TD VALIGN="top"> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Net revenues</B></FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>$</B></FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" ALIGN="right"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>8,082</B></FONT></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;</B></FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>$</B></FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" ALIGN="right"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>8,493</B></FONT></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;</B></FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>&nbsp;</B></FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" ALIGN="right"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>(4.8</B></FONT></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>)%&nbsp;</B></FONT></TD></TR>
</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our 2013 net revenues decreased compared to the
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">year-ago</FONT> period, mainly due to our decision to exit from <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson,</FONT> less favorable market conditions and a lower level of licenses. Net revenues decreased by 4.8% with
a decrease of approximately 9% in average selling prices of which approximately 6% was due to a pure price effect and 3% was due to a less favorable product mix, partially offset by an increase of approximately 4%&nbsp;in volume. In 2013, net
revenues excluding the Wireless product line increased by 3.2%. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">SP&amp;A registered an increase of
approximately 3%, while EPS revenues were down by approximately 15%. Within SP&amp;A, all product lines except AMS increased their revenues with APG up by approximately 7%&nbsp;and IPD up by approximately 3%. Within EPS, Wireless product line sales
registered a decline of approximately 48%, following the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">wind-down</FONT> of the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> joint venture while DCG decreased by approximately 17%. IBP and MMS increased by
about 6% and 19%, respectively, compared to the prior year. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">By market channel, our revenues registered an increase in
Distribution, which was up by approximately 3&nbsp;percentage points, reaching a 26% share of total revenues. By location of shipment, all regions were negatively impacted mainly by the exit of <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson.</FONT> In
2013 and 2012, no customer exceeded 10% of our total net revenues. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">53 </FONT></P>

year, mainly due to the negative impact of selling prices, lower technology licensing revenues and a less favorable product mix, partially offset by improved manufacturing efficiencies, lower unused capacity charges and the absence of the
$53&nbsp;million <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">one-time</FONT> charge related to the 2012 arbitration award to NXP. </FONT></P>

upon project approval, qualifies as funding on the basis of contracts with local government agencies. In 2013, we recognized an income, net, of $95&nbsp;million, increasing compared to an income, net, of $91&nbsp;million in 2012, mainly due to the
sale of businesses and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-current</FONT> assets associated with the Global Navigation Satellite System business in <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> and with the sale of Portland Compiler Group in
ST, partially offset by a lower amount of R&amp;D funding. No grants from the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Nano-2017</FONT> R&amp;D program were recognized in 2013. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT

impairment charges and savings in operating expenses, mainly in R&amp;D, and negatively impacted by a lower level of gross profit primarily due to the reduced level of net revenues. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">SP&amp;A registered operating income of $270&nbsp;million or approximately 6% of revenues, down from $409&nbsp;million or
about 9% of revenues, mainly as a consequence of increased operating expenses also following the reassignment to the segment of some resources from <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> aimed to accelerate and improve product
innovation for the segment. EPS registered an improvement in its operating loss from $883&nbsp;million or approximately 23% of revenues to an operating loss of $399&nbsp;million or about 12% of revenues, mainly due to the reduced level of operating
expenses as a consequence of the exit from <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> and the gain from the sale of businesses only partially offset by reduced gross profit due to the lower revenues level. The segment &#147;Others&#148;
decreased its losses to $336&nbsp;million in 2013, from $1,607&nbsp;million in 2012, mainly due to lower impairment and restructuring charges. </FONT></P>

</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our 2012 net revenues decreased compared to the
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">year-ago</FONT> period, which benefited from more favorable market conditions. Net revenues decreased by 12.8% driven by a decrease of approximately 7%&nbsp;in volume and a decline in average selling prices by
approximately 6%. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Net revenues decreased by approximately 10% for SP&amp;A and 16% for EPS. Within EPS,
Wireless product line revenues registered a decline of approximately 13%. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">By market channel, the relative
breakdown between OEM and Distribution remained similar from one&nbsp;period to the next. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">By location of
shipment, all regions were negatively impacted in terms of revenues by the difficult market conditions. In 2012, no customer exceeded 10% of our total net revenues while the Nokia group of companies accounted for slightly more than 10% of our total
net revenues in 2011. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>Gross profit </I></B></FONT></P>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In 2012, gross margin was 32.8%, decreasing by 390 basis points compared to
the prior year, principally due to the negative impact of declining selling prices, lower sales volume, higher unused capacity charges and $53&nbsp;million NXP arbitration award charge, partially offset by a favorable currency effect and a more
favorable product mix. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>Selling, general and administrative expenses </I></B></FONT></P>

$100&nbsp;million billing of R&amp;D services. On the other side, the 2012 R&amp;D expenses benefited from ongoing cost saving measures and restructuring initiatives mainly in <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> and a more favorable
exchange rate. Our 2012&nbsp;R&amp;D expenses included $3&nbsp;million of <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">share-based</FONT> compensation charges, compared to $8&nbsp;million in 2011. Total R&amp;D expenses were net of research tax credits, which
amounted to $152&nbsp;million in 2012; the amount was $159&nbsp;million in 2011. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As a percentage of revenues,

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In 2012, we recorded $1,376&nbsp;million of impairment, restructuring
charges and other related closure costs, of which: $1,234 million as a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-cash</FONT> impairment on our Wireless goodwill and other intangible assets; $66 million related to the
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> restructuring plan announced in April&nbsp;2012, primarily consisting of employee termination benefits, as well as $2 million impairment charges on
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">long-lived</FONT> assets with no future use; $23 million related to the manufacturing restructuring plan as part of the closure of our Carrollton (Texas) and Phoenix (Arizona) sites of which $21 million was recorded
as an impairment on the Carrollton building and facilities; $21 million related to the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> restructuring plans previously announced in 2011 and 2009; $20 million was recorded in relation to our Digital
restructuring plan announced in October&nbsp;2012 and was composed of employee termination benefits, as well as $7 million impairment charges on intangible assets with no future use; $8 million related to other restructuring initiatives; and a $4
million impairment charge on certain intangibles. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In 2011, we recorded $75 million of impairment,
restructuring charges and other related closure costs, of which: $37 million was recorded in relation to the manufacturing restructuring plan as part of the closure of our Carrollton and Phoenix sites, and was composed of <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">one-time</FONT> termination benefits, as well as other related closure charges, mainly associated with the Phoenix fab, where production was terminated in the first quarter of 2011; $26 million related to the cost savings
plan announced in June 2011 by <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson,</FONT> primarily consisting of employee termination benefits; $7 million related to the workforce reduction plans announced in April and December 2009 by <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson,</FONT> pursuant to the closure of certain locations; and $5 million related to other restructuring initiatives. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>Operating income (loss) </I></B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:12px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">%&nbsp;</FONT></TD></TR>
</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our operating results deteriorated compared to the prior year mainly due to the <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-cash</FONT> impairment charge on Wireless goodwill and other intangible assets, the impact of lower revenues, higher restructuring charges, the NXP arbitration award charge and higher unused capacity charges.
Furthermore, 2011 benefited from a $100&nbsp;million billing of R&amp;D services. This resulted in an operating loss of $2,081&nbsp;million in 2012&nbsp;compared to an operating income of $46&nbsp;million in 2011. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our segments reported a decline in their profitability levels compared to the
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">year-ago</FONT> period, mainly due to lower levels of revenues. SP&amp;A operating income was $409&nbsp;million or approximately 9% of revenues in 2012, down from $757&nbsp;million, or about 15% of 2011 revenues. In
2012, EPS registered an operating loss of $883&nbsp;million or about negative 23% of revenues, down from a $489&nbsp;million operating loss or approximately negative 11% of 2011 revenues. Wireless product line operating loss increased from
$812&nbsp;million in 2011&nbsp;to $885&nbsp;million in 2012, of which the largest part was generated from <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> JVS; 50% of this loss was attributed to Ericsson as noncontrolling interest below operating
income (loss). The segment &#147;Others&#148; increased its losses to $1,607&nbsp;million from $222&nbsp;million in 2011, mainly due to higher impairment and restructuring charges (which accounted for $1,376&nbsp;million in 2012 compared to
$75&nbsp;million in 2011), the higher amount of unused capacity charges (which accounted for $172&nbsp;million in 2012 compared to $149&nbsp;million in 2011) and the NXP arbitration award charge of $54&nbsp;million in 2012. </FONT></P>

<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">%&nbsp;</FONT></TD></TR>
</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In 2012, we reported a net loss of $1,158&nbsp;million, a significant decline compared to
2011 due to the aforementioned factors. In 2011, we reported a net income of $650&nbsp;million. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The 2012 net
loss attributable to the parent company was $(1.31)&nbsp;per share compared to diluted earnings of $0.72 per share in 2011. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In 2012, the impact after tax of impairment, restructuring charges and other related closure costs and other <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">one-time</FONT> items, net of tax, was estimated to be
approximately $(0.98)&nbsp;per share. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Quarterly Results of Operations </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Certain quarterly financial information for the years 2013 and 2012 are set forth below. Such information is derived from
our unaudited Consolidated Financial Statements, prepared on a basis consistent with the Consolidated Financial Statements that include, in our opinion, all normal adjustments necessary for a fair statement of the interim information set forth
therein. Operating results for any quarter are not necessarily indicative of results for any future period. In addition, in view of the significant volatility we have experienced in recent years, the increasingly competitive nature of the markets in
which we operate, the changes in products mix and the currency effects of changes in the composition of sales and production among different geographic regions, we believe that
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">period-to-period</FONT></FONT> comparisons of our operating results should not be relied upon as an indication of future performance. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our quarterly and annual operating results are also affected by a wide variety of other factors that could materially and
adversely affect revenues and profitability or lead to significant variability of operating results, including, among others, capital requirements and the availability of funding, competition, new product development, changes in technology,
manufacturing problems, litigation and possible IP claims. In addition, a number of other factors could lead to fluctuations in operating results, including order cancellations or reduced bookings by key customers or distributors, IP developments,
international events, currency fluctuations, problems in obtaining adequate raw materials on a timely basis, impairment, restructuring charges and other related closure costs, as well as the loss of key personnel. As only a portion of our expenses
varies with our revenues, there can be no assurance that we will be able to reduce costs promptly or adequately in relation to revenue declines to compensate for the effect of any such factors. As a result, unfavorable changes in the above or other
factors have in the past and may in the future adversely affect our operating results. Quarterly results have also been and may be expected to continue to be substantially affected by the cyclical nature of the semiconductor and electronic systems
industries, the speed of some process and manufacturing technology developments, market demand for existing products, the timing and success of new product introductions and the levels of provisions and other unusual charges incurred. Certain
additions of our quarterly results will not total our annual results due to rounding. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">61 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In the fourth quarter of 2013, based upon published industry data by WSTS,
the TAM and the SAM&nbsp;increased <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">year-over-year</FONT></FONT> by approximately 8% and 3%, reaching approximately $80&nbsp;billion and $36&nbsp;billion, while sequentially, the TAM
and the SAM both decreased by about 1% and 2%, respectively. In the fourth quarter of 2013, our average effective exchange rate was approximately $1.34 to &#128;1.00, compared to $1.31 to &#128;1.00 in the third quarter of 2013 and $1.30 to
&#128;1.00 in the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">year-ago</FONT> quarter. Our effective exchange rate reflects actual exchange rate levels combined with the impact of cash flow hedging programs. </FONT></P>

<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>

<TD VALIGN="bottom" ALIGN="right"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>2,162</B></FONT></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;</B></FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>&nbsp;</B></FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" ALIGN="right"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>0.1</B></FONT></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>%&nbsp;</B></FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>&nbsp;</B></FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" ALIGN="right"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>(6.8</B></FONT></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>)%&nbsp;</B></FONT></TD></TR>
</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Year-over-year</FONT></FONT>
comparison </I></B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our fourth quarter of 2013 net revenues were $2,015&nbsp;million, decreasing by 6.8%
compared to the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">year-ago</FONT> period. Excluding the phasing out of the Wireless product line, revenues increased by 3.9% as a result of higher volume by about 6%, partially offset by a decline in average selling
prices by approximately 2%. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">By product segment, SP&amp;A registered an increase of approximately 4%, while
EPS revenues were down by approximately 19%. Within SP&amp;A, all product lines except AMS, which decreased by approximately 15%, increased their revenues with APG up by approximately 22% and IPD up by approximately 7%. Within EPS, Wireless product
line sales registered a decline of approximately 62%, following the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">wind-down</FONT> of the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> joint venture while DCG decreased by approximately 27%. IBP and MMS
increased by about 27%&nbsp;and 22%, respectively, compared to the prior year quarter. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">By market channel, our
revenues registered an increase in Distribution, which was up by approximately 4&nbsp;percentage points, reaching a 27% share of total revenues, compared to 23% in the fourth quarter of 2012. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">By location of shipment, all regions decreased on a year-over-year basis; Japan-Korea, Greater China-South Asia and EMEA
were mainly negatively impacted by the exit of ST-Ericsson while Americas were mainly impacted by a decrease in DCG revenues. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In the fourth quarter of 2013, no customer exceeded 10% of our total net revenues. In the fourth quarter of 2012, the Samsung group of companies accounted for approximately 11% of our net revenues.
</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>Sequential comparison </I></B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On a sequential basis, our revenues were almost flat (+0.1%) with an approximate 3% increase in average selling prices due to a more favorable product mix, offset by an approximate 3% decrease in units
sold. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">By product segment, SP&amp;A revenues increased by approximately 2% mainly driven by a more favorable
product mix, while EPS registered a decrease of approximately 3% primarily due to a decrease in volume. Within the SP&amp;A segment, APG and AMS increased their revenues by approximately 8% and 2%&nbsp;respectively while IPD revenues decreased by
about 2%. Within EPS, all product lines registered a decrease in revenues by approximately 12% for IBP, about 3% for DCG and about 1% for both Wireless product line and MMS. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">By market channel, the fourth quarter of 2013 showed a sequential increase for Distribution, which reached a 27% share of
revenues from the 25% share registered in the third quarter of 2013. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">By location of shipment, all regions
declined sequentially except Greater <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">China-South</FONT> Asia, which increased by approximately 4% on a sequential basis </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Both in the fourth and the third quarters of 2013, no customer exceeded 10% of our total net revenues. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">62 </FONT></P>

<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>

</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Fourth quarter gross margin was 32.9%, increasing on a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">year-over-year</FONT></FONT> basis by approximately 60&nbsp;basis points, mainly due to improved manufacturing efficiencies and lower unused capacity charges, partially offset by declining selling prices and lower
technology licensing revenues compared to the prior year quarter. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On a sequential basis, gross margin in the
fourth quarter increased by 50 basis points, mainly due to improved manufacturing efficiencies, partially offset by a negative currency effect, the negative impact of selling prices and a higher amount of unused capacity charges. </FONT></P>

STYLE="white-space:nowrap">year-over-year</FONT></FONT> and sequentially mainly due to the ST-Ericsson wind-down and the benefits of our cost savings initiatives. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The fourth quarter of 2013 included $3&nbsp;million of <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">share-based</FONT> compensation
charges compared to $1&nbsp;million in the fourth quarter of 2012 and $2&nbsp;million in the third quarter of 2013. Fourth quarter 2013&nbsp;R&amp;D expenses were net of research tax credits, which amounted to $43&nbsp;million, compared to
$42&nbsp;million in the fourth quarter of 2012 and $34&nbsp;million in the third quarter of 2013. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As a
percentage of revenues, fourth quarter 2013 R&amp;D equaled 20.2%, a decrease of approximately 690 basis points compared to the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">year-ago</FONT> period driven by the reduction in our operating expenses and
notwithstanding the level of reduced revenues. On a sequential basis, expenses to revenues percentage decreased by 80 basis points due to decreasing operating expenses. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">63 </FONT></P>
