<TR>
<TD WIDTH="4%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="justify"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">(1)</FONT></P></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Ms.&nbsp;Davidson was appointed as a member of our Supervisory Board on June&nbsp;21, 2013. </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="4%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="justify"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">(2)</FONT></P></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Mr.&nbsp;Bingham&#146;s mandate as member of the Supervisory Board expired on June&nbsp;21, 2013. </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Audit Committee</I>.<I> </I>Our Audit Committee was established in 1996 to assist the Supervisory Board in fulfilling
its oversight responsibilities relating to corporate accounting, reporting practices, and the quality and integrity of our financial reports as well as our auditing practices, legal and regulatory related risks, execution of our auditors&#146;
recommendations regarding corporate auditing rules and the independence of our external auditors. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our Audit
Committee met 10 times during 2013. At many of the Audit Committee&#146;s meetings, the committee received presentations on current financial and accounting issues and had the opportunity to interview our CEO, CFO, General Counsel, Compliance
Officer and external and internal auditors. Our Audit Committee also met with outside U.S. legal counsel to discuss corporate requirements pursuant to NYSE&#146;s corporate governance rules and the Sarbanes Oxley Act. Our Audit Committee also
proceeded with its annual review of our internal audit function. Our Audit Committee reviewed our annual Consolidated Financial Statements in U.S. GAAP for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013, and the results press release was published on
January&nbsp;27, 2014. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our Audit Committee approved the compensation of our external auditors for 2013 and
discussed the scope of their audit, audit related and non-audit related services for 2014. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">At the end of each
quarter, prior to each Supervisory Board meeting to approve our quarterly results and earnings press release, our Audit Committee reviewed our interim financial information and the proposed press release and had the opportunity to raise questions to
management and the independent registered public accounting firm. In addition, our Audit Committee reviewed our quarterly &#147;Operating and Financial Review and Prospects&#148; and Consolidated Financial Statements (and notes thereto) before they
were furnished to the SEC&nbsp;and voluntarily certified by the CEO and the CFO (pursuant to sections&nbsp;302 and 906 of the Sarbanes Oxley Act). Our Audit Committee also reviewed Operating and Financial Review and Prospects and our Consolidated
Financial Statements contained in this <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Form&nbsp;20-F,</FONT> prior to its approval by our Supervisory Board. Furthermore, our Audit Committee monitored our compliance with the European Directive and applicable
provisions of Dutch law that require us to prepare a set of accounts pursuant to IFRS in advance of our Annual General Meeting of Shareholders, which was held on June&nbsp;21, 2013. See &#147;Item&nbsp;3. Key Information &#151; Risk Factors &#151;
Risks Related to Our Operations&#148;. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Also in 2013, our Audit Committee reviewed with our external auditors
our compliance with Section&nbsp;404 of the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Sarbanes-Oxley</FONT> Act. In addition, our Audit Committee regularly discussed the progress of the implementation of internal control over financial reporting and reviewed
management&#146;s conclusions as to the effectiveness of internal control. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As part of each of its quarterly
meetings, our Audit Committee reviewed our financial results as presented by Management and whistleblowing reports, including independent investigative reports provided by internal audit or outside consultants on such matters. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Compensation Committee</I>. Our Compensation Committee was established to advise our Supervisory Board in relation to
the compensation of our President and Chief Executive Officer and sole member of our Managing </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">77 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">
Board, including the variable portion of such compensation based on performance criteria recommended by our Compensation Committee. Our Compensation Committee also reviews the stock based
compensation plans for our senior managers and key employees. Our Compensation Committee met 3&nbsp;times in 2013. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Among its main activities, in 2013 our Compensation Committee: (i)&nbsp;reviewed the objectives met as compared to the performance criteria relating to the CEO bonus for the fiscal year ended on
December&nbsp;31, 2012; (ii)&nbsp;reviewed the performance criteria relating to the CEO bonus for the fiscal year ending on&nbsp;December&nbsp;31, 2013; (iii)&nbsp;reviewed the compensation scheme for members of our Supervisory Board, as proposed by
our Supervisory Board at the annual general meeting of shareholders held on June&nbsp;21, 2013; and (iv)&nbsp;established, on behalf and with the approval of the entire Supervisory Board, the applicable performance criteria, which must be met by
senior managers and selected key employees participating in the employees stock award plans to benefit from such awards. In particular, our Compensation Committee recommended that the performance targets for the bonus of our CEO be based on, among
other factors, the Company&#146;s share price evolution versus SOXX, new major accounts revenues as well as certain financial targets and special programs. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">For the 2013 unvested stock award plan, our Compensation Committee, on behalf, and with the approval, of the entire Supervisory Board, established the applicable performance criteria, which are based on
sales and operating income evolution, as compared against a panel of semiconductor companies, and cash flow targets. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Strategic Committee</I>. Our Strategic Committee was established to advise the Supervisory Board on and monitor key developments within the semiconductor industry and our overall strategy, and is, in
particular, involved in supervising the execution of corporate strategies and in reviewing <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">long-term</FONT> planning and budgeting. Our Strategic Committee met once in 2013. In addition, there were strategic
discussions, many of which occurred at extended Supervisory Board meetings and involved all Supervisory Board members. Among its main activities, our Strategic Committee reviewed prospects and various possible scenarios and opportunities to meet the
challenges of the semiconductor market, including the evaluation of possible divestitures and partnerships to invest in new markets. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee</I>. Our Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee was created to advise the Supervisory Board on the selection criteria and procedures relating to the
appointment of members to our Supervisory Board and Managing Board, and to review principles relating to corporate governance. Our Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee met 5&nbsp;times during 2013&nbsp;to discuss the selection of candidate
members to our Supervisory Board, changes to the Dutch Corporate Governance Code, recent developments in U.S. law regarding corporate governance and preparations for our annual general meeting. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Secretariat and Controllers</I>. Our Supervisory Board appoints a Secretary and Vice Secretary. Furthermore, the
Managing Board makes an Executive Secretary available to our Supervisory Board, who is also appointed by the Supervisory Board. The Secretary, Vice Secretary and Executive Secretary constitute the Secretariat of the Supervisory Board. The mission of
the Secretariat is primarily to organize meetings, to ensure the continuing education and training of our Supervisory Board members and to maintain record keeping. Mr.&nbsp;Bertrand Loubert serves as Secretary, Mr.&nbsp;Luigi Chessa serves as Vice
Secretary and Mr.&nbsp;Philippe Dereeper, our Chief Compliance Officer, serves as Executive Secretary for our Supervisory Board, and for each of the Compensation, Nominating and Corporate Governance and Strategic Committees of our Supervisory Board.
Mr.&nbsp;Willem Toussaint serves as the secretary of our Audit Committee. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our Supervisory Board appoints
two&nbsp;financial experts (&#147;Controllers&#148;). The mission of the Controllers is primarily to assist our Supervisory Board in evaluating our operational and financial performance, business plan, strategic initiatives and the implementation of
Supervisory Board decisions, as well as to review the operational reports provided under the responsibility of the Managing Board. The Controllers generally meet once a month with the management of the Company and report to our Supervisory Board.
The current Controllers are Messrs. Nicolas Manardo and Andrea Novelli. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The STH Shareholders&#146; Agreement
between our principal indirect shareholders contains provisions with respect to the appointment of the Secretary, Vice Secretary and Controllers, which are described in &#147;Item&nbsp;7. Major Shareholders and Related Party Transactions&#148;.
</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>Managing Board </I></B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In accordance with Dutch law, our management is entrusted to the Managing Board under the supervision of our Supervisory Board. Mr.&nbsp;Carlo Bozotti, who was
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">re-appointed</FONT> in 2011 for a three&nbsp;year term to expire at the </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">78 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">
end of our 2014 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders, is currently the sole member of our Managing Board with the function of President and Chief Executive Officer. Since its creation in 1987,
our Managing Board has always been comprised of a sole member. The member of our Managing Board is appointed for a three&nbsp;year term, as described in our Articles of Association, which may be renewed one or more times in accordance with our
Articles of Association upon a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-binding</FONT> proposal by our Supervisory Board at our shareholders&#146; meeting and adoption by a simple majority of the votes cast at the shareholders&#146; meeting where at
least 15% of the issued and outstanding share capital is present or represented. If our Managing Board were to consist of more than one&nbsp;member, our Supervisory Board would appoint one of the members of our Managing Board to be chairman of our
Managing Board for a three&nbsp;year term, as defined in our Articles of Association (upon approval of at least three&nbsp;quarters of the members of our Supervisory Board). In such case, resolutions of our Managing Board would require the approval
of a majority of its members. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our shareholders&#146; meeting may suspend or dismiss one or more members of
our Managing Board at a meeting at which at least one half of the outstanding share capital is present or represented. If a quorum is not present, a further meeting shall be convened, to be held within four weeks after the first meeting, which shall
be entitled, irrespective of the share capital represented, to pass a resolution with regard to the suspension or dismissal of one or more members of our Managing Board. Such a quorum is not required if a suspension or dismissal is proposed by our
Supervisory Board. In that case, a resolution to dismiss or to suspend a member of our Managing Board can be taken by a simple majority of the votes cast at a meeting where at least 15% of our issued and outstanding share capital is present or
represented. Our Supervisory Board may suspend members of our Managing Board, but a shareholders&#146; meeting must be convened within three&nbsp;months after such suspension to confirm or reject the suspension. Our Supervisory Board shall appoint
one or more persons who shall, at any time, in the event of absence or inability to act of all the members of our Managing Board, be temporarily responsible for our management. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Under Dutch law, our Managing Board is entrusted with our general management and the representation of the Company. Our
Managing Board must seek prior approval from our shareholders&#146; meeting for decisions regarding a significant change in the identity or nature of the Company. Under our Articles of Association, our Managing Board must obtain prior approval from
our Supervisory Board for (i)&nbsp;all proposals to be submitted to a vote at a shareholders&#146; meeting; (ii)&nbsp;the formation of all companies, acquisition or sale of any participation, and conclusion of any cooperation and participation
agreement; (iii)&nbsp;all of our <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">multi-year</FONT> plans and the budget for the coming&nbsp;year, covering investment policy, policy regarding R&amp;D, as well as commercial policy and objectives, general financial
policy, and policy regarding personnel; and (iv)&nbsp;all acts, decisions or operations covered by the foregoing and constituting a significant change with respect to decisions already taken by our Supervisory Board. In addition, under our Articles
of Association, our Supervisory Board and our shareholders&#146; meeting may specify by resolution certain additional actions by our Managing Board that require its prior approval. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In accordance with our Corporate Governance Charter, the sole member of our Managing Board and our senior managers may
not serve on the board of a public company without the prior approval of our Supervisory Board. Pursuant to the charter adopted by our Supervisory Board, the sole member of our Managing Board must inform our Supervisory Board of any (potential)
conflict of interest and pursuant to such charter and Dutch law, any Managing Board resolution regarding a transaction in relation to which the sole member of our Managing Board has a conflict of interest must be approved and adopted by our
Supervisory Board. Should our entire Supervisory Board also have a conflict of interest, the resolution must be adopted by our shareholders&#146; meeting pursuant to Dutch law. We are not aware of any potential conflicts of interests between the
private interest or other duties of our sole Management Board member and our senior managers and their duties to our Company. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Pursuant to the charter adopted by our Supervisory Board, the following decisions by our Managing Board with regard to the Company and any of our direct or indirect subsidiaries (an &#147;ST Group
Company&#148;) require prior approval from our Supervisory Board: (i)&nbsp;any modification of our or any ST Group Company&#146;s Articles of Association or other constitutional documents, other than those of wholly owned subsidiaries; (ii)&nbsp;any
change in our or any ST Group Company&#146;s authorized share capital or any issue, acquisition or disposal by us of our own shares, or any ST Group Company&#146;s shares, or change in share rights or issue of any instruments granting an interest in
our or an ST Group Company&#146;s capital or profits other than those of our wholly owned subsidiaries; (iii)&nbsp;any liquidation or dissolution of us or any ST Group Company or the disposal of all or a substantial and material part of our business
or assets, or those of any ST Group Company, or of any shares in any such ST Group Company; (iv)&nbsp;any merger, acquisition or joint venture agreement (and, if substantial and material, any agreement relating to IP) or formation of a new company
to which we or any ST Group Company is, or is proposed to be, a party, as well as the formation of new companies by us or any ST Group Company (with the understanding that only acquisitions above $25&nbsp;million per transaction are subject to prior
Supervisory Board </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">79 </FONT></P>

approval); (v)&nbsp;approval of our draft Consolidated Balance Sheets and Consolidated Financial Statements, as well as our and our subsidiaries&#146; profit distribution policies;
(vi)&nbsp;entering into any agreement that may qualify as a related party transaction, including any agreement between us or any ST Group Company and ST Holding, FT1CI, Ministero dell&#146;Economia e delle Finanze, Bpifrance or CEA; (vii)&nbsp;the
key parameters of our <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">five-year</FONT> plans and our consolidated annual budgets, as well as any significant modifications to said plans and budgets, or any one of the matters set forth in our Articles of Association
and not included in the approved plans or budgets; (viii)&nbsp;approval of operations of exceptional importance which have to be submitted for Supervisory Board prior approval even if their financing was already provided for in the approved annual
budget; (ix)&nbsp;approval of our quarterly and annual Consolidated Financial Statements prepared in accordance with U.S. GAAP and semi-annual and annual accounts using IFRS, prior to submission for shareholder adoption; and (x)&nbsp;the exercise of
any shareholder right in an ST joint venture company, which is a company (a)&nbsp;with respect to which we hold directly or indirectly either a minority equity position in excess of 25% or a majority position without the voting power to adopt
extraordinary resolutions, or (b)&nbsp;in which we directly or indirectly participate and such participation has a value of at least one third of our total assets according to the Consolidated Balance Sheets and notes thereto in our most recently
adopted (statutory) annual accounts. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>Senior Management </I></B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our senior managers support our Managing Board in its management of the Company, without prejudice to our Managing
Board&#146;s ultimate responsibility. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In August 2013, we introduced a new, simplified organization. Since
that date, our organizational chart is as follows: </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:12px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center">


<IMG SRC="g622882g35q23.jpg" ALT="LOGO">
 </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As a company committed to good governance, we hold several corporate meetings on a
regular basis. Such meetings, which involve the participation of several of our senior management, include: </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Corporate Operations Reviews (COR)</B>, which meets twice per quarter to review monthly results and <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">short-term</FONT> forecasts. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Corporate Staff Meeting</B>, which meets once per
quarter to review the business in its entirety and to plan and forecast for the next quarter and beyond. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Corporate Strategic Committee</B>, which meets six times per year, sets corporate policy, coordinates strategies of
our various functions and drives major cross functional programs. </FONT></P>

<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Mr.&nbsp;Mario Arlati was Chief Financial Officer until August&nbsp;2013. </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Biographies of our Current Senior Management </I></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Carlo Bozotti is President and Chief Executive Officer of ST and has held this position since March&nbsp;2005. He is the
Sole Member of the Managing Board and chairs the Company&#146;s Corporate Strategic Committee. Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti joined SGS-ATES (later renamed SGS Microelettronica), a predecessor company to STMicroelectronics, in 1977. Ten&nbsp;years later, when
SGS Microelettronica of Italy merged with Thomson Semiconducteurs of France to form a new European champion, which is ST today and is among the leading semiconductor companies worldwide, Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti became General Manager of the Telecom Product
Division. Subsequently, he was promoted to Director of Corporate Strategic Marketing and Key Accounts and, later, to Corporate Vice President, Marketing and Sales, Americas. In 1994, Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti was appointed Corporate Vice President for Europe
and the Headquarters Regions, overseeing the Company&#146;s sales in Europe, as well as sales to key customers and strategic marketing worldwide. From 1998 to 2005, Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti served as Corporate Vice President and General Manager of the
Memory Products Group. Leveraging ST&#146;s deep understanding of client and market needs, through the years Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti has contributed to forging a number of strategic customer alliances and joint ventures with other industry leaders in
different market segments. In late 2012, Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti announced a strategic plan realigning the Company with changing market dynamics and announcing the decision to exit from ST-Ericsson after a transition period. The new ST focuses on
two&nbsp;key pillars &#151; Sense and Power and Automotive products and Embedded Processing Solutions &#151; with five growth drivers: MEMS and sensors, smart power, automotive products, microcontrollers, and application processors including digital
consumer. In 2011, Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti began a second (non-consecutive) term as the President of the European Semiconductor Industry Association (ESIA). Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti graduated with a degree in Electronic Engineering from the University of Pavia,
Italy. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Jean-Marc</FONT> Chery is Executive Vice President, General Manager,
Embedded Processing Solutions (EPS) at ST, and has held this position since May&nbsp;2013. He also holds overall responsibility for the Technology R&amp;D and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Front-End</FONT> Manufacturing of EPS, as well as the
Packaging&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Test Manufacturing and Product Quality Excellence for the whole Company. Mr.&nbsp;Chery is Vice Chairman of our Corporate Strategic Committee. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">82 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">
Mr.&nbsp;Chery began his career in the Quality organization of Matra, the French engineering group. In 1986, he joined Thomson Semiconducteurs, which subsequently became ST, and held various
management positions in product planning, rising to lead ST&#146;s wafer fabs in Tours, France (1993), and later in Rousset, France (2001). In 2005, Mr.&nbsp;Chery led the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">company-wide</FONT> 150-mm <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">wafer-production</FONT> restructuring program before taking charge of ST&#146;s <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Front-End</FONT> Manufacturing operations in Asia Pacific. In 2008, he was promoted Chief Technology Officer
and assumed additional responsibilities for Manufacturing and Quality in 2011 and the Digital Product Sector in 2012. Mr.&nbsp;Chery sits on the boards of <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson</FONT> SA, CATRENE, the European microelectronics
R&amp;D program, and AENEAS (Association for European NanoElectronics Activities). Mr.&nbsp;Chery graduated with a degree in Engineering from the ENSAM engineering school in Paris, France. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Carlo Ferro is Chief Financial Officer, Executive Vice President Finance, Legal, Infrastructure and Services and has held
the CFO position since May&nbsp;2003, with temporary suspension during his tenure at <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson,</FONT> where he first served as Chief Operating Officer (February&nbsp;2012 <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">-</FONT>
March&nbsp;2013) and then was appointed President and Chief Executive Officer in April&nbsp;2013 to lead the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">re-organization</FONT> to split up the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">joint-venture</FONT> business and
resources to the two&nbsp;shareholders and wind-down the JV. Since August&nbsp;2013, his overall responsibilities at ST encompass, in addition to Finance and Control, Central Operational Planning, Global Procurement, Legal, Intellectual Property,
Compliance, Information and Communication Technology, Investor Relations, and Public Affairs in Italy. Mr.&nbsp;Ferro is a member of our Corporate Strategic Committee. He also maintains the role of President and CEO of
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">ST-Ericsson.</FONT> From 1992 to 1996, Mr.&nbsp;Ferro gained extensive experience in Planning and Control, Corporate Finance and M&amp;A at Finmeccanica, the leading Italian
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">high-tech</FONT> engineering and manufacturing group and a former shareholder of STMicroelectronics. Over the next three&nbsp;years he held executive positions for Elsag Bailey Process Automation NV, a global leader
in process control listed at NYSE, first as Vice President for Strategic Planning, and later as Vice President for Planning and Control and Principal Financial Officer. In 1999, Mr.&nbsp;Ferro joined ST as Group Vice President Corporate Finance,
overseeing finance and accounting for all the Company&#146;s worldwide affiliates, tax planning, internal control, internal audit, and finance for M&amp;A. In 2002, he became Deputy CFO, and was promoted to Chief Financial Officer in 2003.
Mr.&nbsp;Ferro sits on the Board of Directors of STS, the Company&#146;s manufacturing joint venture in China, and holds board memberships at ST&#146;s affiliates in France and Italy. He served as Chairman of Incard, sole Managing Director of ST
Service Srl and as board member and Chair of the Audit Committee of ST-Ericsson. He graduated in Business and Economics from the LUISS Guido Carli University in Rome, where he served as a professor of Planning and Control until 1996 and as an
associate professor of Finance from 2008 through 2011. He is a Certified Public Accountant in Italy. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Mario
Arlati is Executive Vice President, Strategies and Business Management for the Company&#146;s Sense&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Power and Automotive Products (SP&amp;A) Segment and has held this position since August&nbsp;2013. Mr.&nbsp;Arlati is a member of
our Corporate Strategic Committee. Mr.&nbsp;Arlati started his professional career at <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">SGS-ATES,</FONT> a predecessor company of STMicroelectronics. He was an integral member of the teams that managed the 1987 merger
of SGS Microelettronica and Thomson Semiconducteurs, and later, in 1994, ST&#146;s Initial Public Offering on the NYSE and Euronext Paris, followed in 1998 by ST&#146;s listing on the Borsa Italiana. Mr.&nbsp;Arlati&#146;s career has covered all of
the various functions including Accounting, Business Control, Finance, and Consolidation Reporting, in positions of increasing responsibility. He was promoted to Corporate Controller and later became Chief Accounting Officer and Head of External
Reporting. He also participated in the establishment of the ST Foundation, an independent charitable organization, serving as a Director since its inception. From February&nbsp;2012 to July&nbsp;2013, Mr.&nbsp;Arlati served as Chief Financial
Officer of ST. He graduated with a degree in Business and Economics from Universit&agrave; Cattolica in Milan, Italy. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Georges Auguste is Executive Vice President and General Manager of ST&#146;s Packaging and Test Manufacturing organization and has held this position since May&nbsp;2011. Mr.&nbsp;Auguste started his
career in semiconductors with Philips as a technical manager. He joined Thomson Semiconducteurs, a predecessor company to STMicroelectronics, in 1986, and two&nbsp;years later was appointed General Manager of the manufacturing facility in Nancy,
France. From 1990, Mr.&nbsp;Auguste served as Managing Director of the Company&#146;s operations in Morocco. In 1997, he was appointed Director of the Total Quality and Environment Group, and, in 1999, he was promoted to Corporate Vice President,
responsible for implementing ST&#146;s goals to reduce consumption of natural resources and further improve quality. In 2005, Mr.&nbsp;Auguste&#146;s mission was enlarged to cover the coordination of ST&#146;s corporate responsibility strategy,
encompassing social, ethical and environmental aspects. In 2008, he became Executive Vice President, Director of Product Quality Excellence, addressing product quality matters throughout the Company&#146;s operations. Mr.&nbsp;Auguste graduated with
an engineering degree from the Ecole Sup&eacute;rieure d&#146;Electricit&eacute; in Paris and holds the diploma of the &#147;Institut d&#146;Administration des Entreprises&#148; (Institute of Business Administration). </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">83 </FONT></P>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Eric Aussedat is Executive Vice President and General Manager of Imaging,
BiCMOS ASIC and Silicon Photonics Group and has managed these responsibilities since February&nbsp;2012. Mr.&nbsp;Aussedat joined Thomson Semiconducteurs, a predecessor company to ST, as Product Engineer in 1981. He held various positions in product
engineering and planning and was promoted Planning Manager of the Video Products Group in 1986. Later on, Mr.&nbsp;Aussedat was appointed to manage the product and manufacturing planning operations of INMOS, a UK company acquired by ST in 1989.
Subsequently, he supervised the Engineering and Test Strategy for the Programmable Product Group before his promotion to the Head of ST&#146;s Microcontroller Division in 1995. From 2000 to 2004, Mr.&nbsp;Aussedat led the TV and Display Division,
and he became General Manager of ST&#146;s Cellular Communication Division in 2005. Two&nbsp;years later, Mr.&nbsp;Aussedat was appointed General Manager of the Imaging Division, which has significantly strengthened its profitability under his
direction. Mr.&nbsp;Aussedat graduated with a degree in Electronic Engineering from the Institut National Polytechnique in Grenoble and earned a diploma from the Institut d&#146;Administration des Entreprises of Grenoble. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Orio Bellezza is Executive Vice President and General Manager of <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Front-End</FONT>
Manufacturing&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Technology R&amp;D for the Company&#146;s Sense&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Power and Automotive Products (SP&amp;A) Segment. He has been responsible for <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Front-End</FONT> Manufacturing since 2008
and assumed additional responsibility for technology R&amp;D for automotive and industrial&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;multisegment products in February&nbsp;2012. He is a member of our Corporate Strategic Committee. Mr.&nbsp;Bellezza joined <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">SGS-ATES,</FONT> a predecessor company to STMicroelectronics, in 1984. He soon moved to the Company&#146;s Central R&amp;D organization and participated in several key projects, including the introduction of process
technology modules for manufacturing <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">sub-micron</FONT> <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-volatile</FONT> memories. In 1996, Mr.&nbsp;Bellezza was appointed Director of ST&#146;s R&amp;D facility in Agrate and led
its upgrade and expansion into the Company&#146;s development center for <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-volatile</FONT> memory and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">smart-power</FONT> technologies. In 2002, he became Vice President of Central
R&amp;D, and in 2005, was appointed to Vice President and Assistant General Manager of <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Front-End</FONT> Technology and Manufacturing. He has published technical papers and earned several patents in <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-volatile</FONT> memories. Mr.&nbsp;Bellezza graduated with a degree in Chemistry from the University of Milan (Universit&agrave; degli Studi di Milano), Italy. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Gian Luca Bertino is Executive Vice President and General Manager of ST&#146;s Digital Convergence Group and has held
this position since February&nbsp;2012. Mr.&nbsp;Bertino started his professional career with Olivetti Personal Computers, where he rose through the ranks to Head of Development, Portable PCs. Bertino joined
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">SGS-Thomson</FONT> Microelectronics (now STMicroelectronics) in 1997 as a Market Development Manager. The following year he was appointed Director of the Computer and Consumer Business Unit, responsible for sales and
marketing in Europe, and was promoted to Europe Region Vice President, Computer and Consumer, in 2000. In 2003, Mr.&nbsp;Bertino joined ST&#146;s Telecommunications, Peripherals and Automotive Group as Vice President and General Manager of the Data
Storage Division, where he strengthened the Company&#146;s partnerships in the storage segment. In 2005, Mr.&nbsp;Bertino was promoted to Corporate Vice President and General Manager of ST&#146;s Computer Product Group, and in 2008,
Mr.&nbsp;Bertino&#146;s group was expanded to include the Communication Infrastructure organization. Mr.&nbsp;Bertino graduated with a degree in Electronic Engineering from the Polytechnic of Turin, Italy. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Philippe Brun is a Corporate Vice President in charge of ST&#146;s Human Resources&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Sustainable
Development. Responsible for HR at ST since August&nbsp;2012, his mission was expanded to cover the Company&#146;s social responsibility, as well as environment, health, and safety in August&nbsp;2013. Mr.&nbsp;Brun started his career at the
Pechiney Group (now Rio Tinto). In 1986, he joined Thomson Semiconducteurs, a predecessor to STMicroelectronics, as a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">back-end</FONT> process engineer. From 1989 to 1996, Mr.&nbsp;Brun managed Human Resources at the
Grenoble, France site and served as Site Director at the Company&#146;s St. Genis facility (France). In 1996,&nbsp;he was promoted to Human Resources Director responsible for over 10,000 employees in ST&#146;s manufacturing organization worldwide.
From 1999 to 2010, Mr.&nbsp;Brun served as Fab Operations and Site Director at ST&#146;s plant in Rousset, France. In January&nbsp;2011, he was appointed Group VP for execution excellence in ST&#146;s
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Front-End</FONT> Manufacturing organization. Mr.&nbsp;Brun graduated with an engineering degree from the Ecole Nationale Sup&eacute;rieure d&#146;Arts et M&eacute;tiers (ENSAM) in France and holds a Master degree in
Aerospace engineering from the University of Colorado and a management degree from the IFG School of Business (France). </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Marco Cassis is an Executive Vice President of ST and President of the Company&#146;s Japan and Korea Region. Mr.&nbsp;Cassis has led ST&#146;s operations in Japan since 2005 and his mandate was expanded
to include Korea after the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">re-organization</FONT> of the Company&#146;s regional structure in January&nbsp;2010. Mr.&nbsp;Cassis joined <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">SGS-Thomson</FONT> Microelectronics (now
STMicroelectronics) as a car radio chip designer in 1987. Six&nbsp;years later, he moved to Japan to help expand the Company&#146;s audio business with major Japanese players and contributed to the establishment of ST&#146;s strategic alliance with
Pioneer in the late 1990s. In 2000, Mr.&nbsp;Cassis took charge of the </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">84 </FONT></P>

Audio Business Unit and he was subsequently promoted to Director of Audio and Automotive Group, responsible for design, marketing, sales, application support and customer service. In 2004,
Mr.&nbsp;Cassis was named Vice President of Marketing for automotive, computer peripheral, and telecom products. In 2005, he advanced to VP Automotive Segment Group and joined the Board of STMicroelectronics K.K., the Japanese subsidiary.
Mr.&nbsp;Cassis graduated with a degree in Electronic Engineering from the Polytechnic of Milan, Italy. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Andrea Cuomo is Executive Vice President, Advanced Systems Technology (AST) and Special Projects, and has held this
position since January&nbsp;2012. He also serves as Chairman of the Board at 3Sun, ST&#146;s photovoltaic joint venture with Enel Green Power and Sharp. Mr.&nbsp;Cuomo joined SGS Microelettronica, a predecessor company to STMicroelectronics, in
1983, and rose to become Vice President for the Headquarters Region in 1994. In 1998, he created the AST group, a key organization in the development of ST&#146;s system knowledge and advanced architectures. In 2002, Mr.&nbsp;Cuomo was promoted to
Corporate Vice President and AST General Manager, and took on further responsibilities as Chief Strategy Officer in 2005. In 2008,&nbsp;Mr.&nbsp;Cuomo was appointed Executive Vice President, General Manager of Sales and Marketing for Europe and AST
and his portfolio was later expanded to include the Middle East and Africa. His board memberships include the International Advisory Board at the HEC Business School in Paris, the International Advisory Board of
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Nano-Tera,</FONT> a Swiss National program for Nanotechnologies, and TTFactor, the IP utilization arm of the Istituto Europeo di Oncologia. Mr.&nbsp;Cuomo studied Nuclear Science at the Polytechnic of Milan.
</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Claude Dardanne is Executive Vice President and General Manager of ST&#146;s Microcontroller,
Memory&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Secure MCU Group and has held this position since January&nbsp;2007. He is a member of our Corporate Strategic Committee. Mr.&nbsp;Dardanne started his career with Thomson Semiconducteurs, a predecessor company to ST. From
1982, he was responsible for the marketing of microcontroller and microprocessor products. Between 1989 and 1994, Mr.&nbsp;Dardanne served as Marketing Director at Apple Computer, France, and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Alcatel-Mietec,</FONT>
Belgium, covering markets such as Education, Banking, as well as Automotive and Industrial. In 1994, he rejoined ST as Director of Central Marketing for the Memory Products Group. In 1998,&nbsp;Mr.&nbsp;Dardanne became Head of the EEPROM
(Electronically Erasable Programmable <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Read-Only</FONT> Memory) Division and was promoted to Group Vice President and General Manager of the Serial <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Non-Volatile</FONT> Memories
Division in 2002. Two&nbsp;years later, he was appointed Group Deputy General Manager and Head of the Smart Card Division. Mr.&nbsp;Dardanne graduated with a degree in Electronic Engineering from the Ecole Sup&eacute;rieure d&#146;Ing&eacute;nieurs
en G&eacute;nie Electrique in Rouen, France. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Lorenzo Grandi is Corporate Vice President, Corporate Control
and has held his position since February&nbsp;2012. Mr.&nbsp;Grandi joined ST in 1987 as a process engineer working on BCD Technology development. In 1990, he moved to the Memory Product Group as Financial Analyst. In 1995, Mr.&nbsp;Grandi was
promoted to the position of Group Controller of the Memory Product Group contributing to the expansion of the Flash/Memory business. In 2005, Mr.&nbsp;Grandi joined Corporate Finance with the responsibility for Budgeting and Reporting. He also
contributed to the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">carve-out</FONT> and deconsolidation of the ST Flash memory business. Mr.&nbsp;Grandi graduated cum laude in Physics from the University of Modena and holds a Master of Business Administration from
SDA Bocconi Milano. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Paul Grimme is Executive Vice President and General Manager,
Sales&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Marketing, for ST&#146;s Europe, Middle East and Africa Region, and has held this position since January&nbsp;2012. Mr.&nbsp;Grimme is a member of our Corporate Strategic Committee. Mr.&nbsp;Grimme began his career at Motorola,
where he held positions of increasing responsibility in product engineering, marketing and operations management. He served as Corporate Vice President and General Manager of the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">8/16-bit</FONT> Products Division. In
1999, Mr.&nbsp;Grimme was promoted to Vice President and General Manager of the Advanced Vehicle Systems Division. He was later appointed Senior Vice President of the Transportation and Standard Products Group and continued in that role at Freescale
Semiconductor after Motorola spun off its semiconductor business. Mr.&nbsp;Grimme also served as Senior Vice President and General Manager of Freescale Semiconductor&#146;s Microcontroller Solutions Group. Mr.&nbsp;Grimme joined ST in early 2009 and
later that year he was appointed Executive Vice President and General Manager of STMicroelectronics&#146; Automotive Product Group. Mr.&nbsp;Grimme graduated from the University of Nebraska (Lincoln) with a degree in Electrical Engineering and from
the University of Texas (Austin) with a Master of Business Administration. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Fabio Gualandris is an Executive
Vice President of ST in charge of Product Quality Excellence and has held this position since February&nbsp;2011. Mr.&nbsp;Gualandris joined the R&amp;D organization of SGS Microelettronica, a predecessor company to ST, in 1984, and was promoted to
R&amp;D Director of Operations in 1989. In 1996,&nbsp;Mr.&nbsp;Gualandris became Automotive Business Unit Director, focusing on product quality and development. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">85 </FONT></P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">
After two&nbsp;years in the U.S. as President and CEO of Semitool, a semiconductor manufacturing equipment vendor, he rejoined ST in 2000 as Group VP responsible for the RAM/PSRAM Product
Division and the Flash Automotive Business Unit. In 2005, Mr.&nbsp;Gualandris was appointed CEO of ST Incard, an ST <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">smart-card</FONT> subsidiary. Two&nbsp;years later, he contributed to the carve out of ST&#146;s
Flash Memory Group and subsequently joined Numonyx, the joint venture with Intel, as VP and Supply Chain General Manager. Mr.&nbsp;Gualandris has authored several technical and managerial papers, holds some international patents, and served as a
board member in Incard SA, ST Incard, and the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Numonyx-Hynix</FONT> joint venture in China. He also served as Board member and President of Numonyx Italy. Mr.&nbsp;Gualandris graduated in Physics from the University
of Milan. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Fran&ccedil;ois Guibert is Executive Vice President of ST and President of the Company&#146;s
Greater China-South Asia Region. Guibert has led ST&#146;s operations in Asia Pacific since 2006; his responsibilities were expanded to include Greater China in 2010. Guibert joined Thomson Semiconducteurs, a predecessor company to ST, in 1981,
after three years at Texas Instruments. He was appointed Director of Semicustom Business for Asia Pacific in 1987 and later became President of ST&#146;s Taiwan operations. Guibert also held senior positions in Corporate Business Development and
Investor Relations. In 2005, he was promoted to Corporate Vice President, Emerging Markets. Guibert serves as Director of ST&#146;s JV with Shenzhen High Tech Industrial Company. He chairs the EU-ASEAN Business Council, the Board of Advisors for the
Singapore Semiconductor Industry Association, and sits on boards at EuroCham in Singapore and Alliance Francaise de Singapour. Guibert previously chaired the Board at Veredus Laboratories, ST&#146;s Singapore-based life-sciences subsidiary, and was
a board member at the Singapore Economic Development Board. He was decorated Knight of the National Order of Merit in France in 2009. Fran&ccedil;ois Guibert was born in Beziers, France, in 1953, and graduated with a degree in Electronic Engineering
from Ecole Centrale Marseille, France. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Joel Hartmann is Executive Vice President of ST, <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Front-End</FONT> Manufacturing and Technology R&amp;D, Embedded Processing Solutions (EPS). He has managed these responsibilities for ST&#146;s digital products since February&nbsp;2012. Mr.&nbsp;Hartmann is also in charge
of the Company&#146;s manufacturing operations in Crolles, France. From 1979 to 2000, Mr.&nbsp;Hartmann worked at <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">CEA-Leti,</FONT> an <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">applied-research</FONT> center for
microelectronics, information and healthcare technologies in Grenoble, France. In 2000, he joined ST as Director of the Crolles2 Alliance, the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">large-scale</FONT> semiconductor manufacturing R&amp;D initiative of
STMicroelectronics, NXP and Freescale Semiconductor. In 2008, Mr.&nbsp;Hartmann was promoted to Group Vice President and Director of Advanced CMOS Logic&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Derivative Technologies. In 2010, he gained additional responsibilities as a <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">co-leader</FONT> of the Semiconductor Research and Development Center in Fishkill, NY, within the IBM ISDA Technology Alliance for the development of advanced CMOS process. Mr.&nbsp;Hartmann sits on the Board of the SOI
Industry Consortium Initiative and is a Member of the IEEE Electron Device Society. He has filed 15&nbsp;patents on semiconductor technology and devices and authored 10 publications in this field to date. Mr.&nbsp;Hartmann graduated from the Ecole
Nationale Sup&eacute;rieure de Physique de Grenoble (ENSPG) with a degree in Physics. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Otto Kosgalwies is
Executive Vice President of ST in charge of Company Infrastructures and Services and has held this position since November&nbsp;2004. Central Planning was added to his mandate in 2008. Mr.&nbsp;Kosgalwies is a member of our Corporate Strategic
Committee. Mr.&nbsp;Kosgalwies joined SGS Bauelemente, a predecessor company to STMicroelectronics, in 1984. He took charge of central marketing for European distribution in 1992, and three&nbsp;years later, he began to oversee the regional sales
and marketing operations. In 1997, Mr.&nbsp;Kosgalwies was appointed Managing Director of the Company&#146;s site in Munich, Germany. In 2001,&nbsp;he was promoted to Group Vice President for Sales&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Marketing in Europe and General
Manager of Supply Chain Management, responsible for the effective flow of goods and information from suppliers to end users at the global level. Mr.&nbsp;Kosgalwies graduated with a degree in Business and Economics from the <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Ludwigs-Maximilian</FONT> University in Munich. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Robert Krysiak is an Executive
Vice President of ST and President of the Company&#146;s Americas Region and has held this position since January&nbsp;2010. He also chairs ST&#146;s Task Force on Electronic Manufacturing Services, the Worldwide Computer Market Program and the
Worldwide Medical Program. Mr.&nbsp;Krysiak started his professional career in 1983 with INMOS, a company acquired by <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">SGS-Thomson</FONT> Microelectronics (now STMicroelectronics) in 1989. He formed and led a CPU
design group since 1992, and in 1997 he was appointed Group Vice President and General Manager of ST&#146;s STAR division, which incorporated <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">16/32/64-bit</FONT> microcontrollers and DSP products. Two&nbsp;years
later he became Group Vice President responsible for micro cores development, including advanced <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap"><FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">System-on-Chip</FONT></FONT> products for the digital consumer market. In
2001,&nbsp;Mr.&nbsp;Krysiak took charge of ST&#146;s DVD division. In 2004, he was promoted to Marketing Director for the Home, Personal, and Communications sector, the Company&#146;s largest product organization at the time. In
</FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">86 </FONT></P>

General Manager. Mr.&nbsp;Krysiak graduated from Cardiff University, UK, with a degree in Electronics and holds an MBA from the University of Bath, UK. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Philippe Magarshack is Executive Vice President of ST in charge of Design Enablement&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Services. He has had these responsibilities since March&nbsp;2012. From 1985 to 1989,
Mr.&nbsp;Magarshack worked as a microprocessor designer at AT&amp;T Bell Labs in the USA. In 1989, he joined <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Thomson-CSF</FONT> in Grenoble, France, and took responsibility for libraries and ASIC design kits for the
military market. In 1994,&nbsp;Mr.&nbsp;Magarshack joined the Central R&amp;D Group of <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">SGS-THOMSON</FONT> Microelectronics (now STMicroelectronics), where he has held several roles in CAD and Libraries management for
advanced <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">integrated-circuit</FONT> manufacturing processes. In 2005, Mr.&nbsp;Magarshack was promoted to Group Vice President and General Manager of Central CAD and Design Solutions at ST&#146;s Technology R&amp;D
and Manufacturing organization. Mr.&nbsp;Magarshack is ST&#146;s Enablement Executive at the IBM ISDA Technology Alliance for the development of advanced CMOS process. He sits on the boards of Silicon Integration Initiative (Si2) and ENSIMAG
Engineering School in Grenoble. Mr.&nbsp;Magarshack graduated with an engineering degree in Physics from Ecole Polytechnique, Palaiseau, France, and with an Electronics Engineering degree from Ecole Nationale Sup&eacute;rieure des
T&eacute;l&eacute;communications in Paris, France. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Marco Monti is Executive Vice President and General
Manager of ST&#146;s Automotive Product Group and has held this position since January&nbsp;2012. Mr.&nbsp;Monti joined ST in Central R&amp;D in 1986 and transferred to the Automotive Division in 1988, where he designed automotive ICs incorporating <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">smart-power</FONT> technologies. He moved to Japan in 1990 working on a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">co-development</FONT> activity designing a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">noise-reduction</FONT> system for audio
applications. Subsequently, Mr.&nbsp;Monti transferred into marketing, contributing to the expansion of ST&#146;s Automotive business in Japan. In 2000, he became the marketing manager for the ST Automotive Division. Two&nbsp;years later,
Mr.&nbsp;Monti started the automotive microprocessor business and in 2004 was promoted to Division General Manager for Powertrain, Safety and Chassis products. In 2009, he took responsibility for the Automotive Electronics Division inside ST&#146;s
Automotive Product Group. Mr.&nbsp;Monti graduated cum laude in Electronic Engineering from the Polytechnic of Milan, Italy, and two&nbsp;years later from the University of Pavia, Italy, with a PhD in Electronics. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Carmelo Papa is Executive Vice President, General Manager of the Industrial&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Power Discrete Group and has
held this position since January&nbsp;2012. He also leads ST&#146;s Online Marketing operations and holds overall responsibility for the System Lab, part of ST&#146;s Central Labs organization. Mr.&nbsp;Papa sits on the Board of Directors of ST New
Ventures SA and is a member of our Corporate Strategic Committee. Mr.&nbsp;Papa started his professional career with International Computers Limited. He joined SGS Microelettronica, a predecessor company to STMicroelectronics, in 1983, and
three&nbsp;years later was promoted to Director of Product Marketing and Customer Service for Transistors and Standard ICs. In 2000, Mr.&nbsp;Papa was appointed Corporate Vice President, responsible for ST&#146;s sales and marketing in Emerging
Markets. In 2005, he was chosen to lead the Micro, Power and Analog Group and his mandate was expanded in 2007 as head of the Industrial&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Multisegment Sector. Mr.&nbsp;Papa is currently serving his second term as Chairman of the
European Platform on Smart Systems, an <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">industry-driven</FONT> initiative focused on innovation in nanotechnologies and smart systems integration. Mr.&nbsp;Papa graduated with a degree in Nuclear Physics from the
University of Catania. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Georges Penalver is Chief Strategy Officer of ST, Executive Vice President Strategy,
Communication, Human Resources and Quality and has held this position since August&nbsp;2013. His overall responsibilities include Corporate Strategy and Development, Corporate Communication, Human Resources, Corporate Security, Product Quality
Excellence, Public Affairs in France and EU, and ST New Ventures. He is a member of our Corporate Strategic Committee. Mr.&nbsp;Penalver started his career in 1980 with Sagem, where he developed the Broadband Communications Business, overseeing the
launch of telecommunication products, the international industrial deployments, and the development of global sales networks. Mr.&nbsp;Penalver was appointed to Sagem&#146;s Management Board in 2001 and served as Deputy CEO, pushing the mass
development of mobile and Internet services. In 2005, he joined the France Telecom Orange Group as Deputy CEO for Strategy and Business Development, responsible, at the group level, for product marketing and management of services in France Telecom
Orange, product creation and development for the entire group, and Orange Labs&#146; activities worldwide. In 2011, Mr.&nbsp;Penalver used his extensive experience to become a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">co-founder</FONT> and managing partner
of CathayaCapital Fund. Mr.&nbsp;Penalver serves as EMEA Regional Leadership Director for the Global Semiconductor Alliance. He is a Knight of the French &#147;Ordre National du M&eacute;rite&#148;. Mr.&nbsp;Penalver holds a degree from the Ecole
Nationale Sup&eacute;rieure d&#146;Arts et M&eacute;tiers (Gold) and from the Ecole Nationale Sup&eacute;rieure des T&eacute;l&eacute;communications in Paris. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Benedetto Vigna is Executive Vice President, General Manager of the Analog, MEMS&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Sensors Group, and has held this position since September&nbsp;2011. Mr.&nbsp;Vigna is a member of our
Corporate Strategic Committee. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">87 </FONT></P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">
In 1995, Mr.&nbsp;Vigna joined ST&#146;s R&amp;D Labs and launched the Company&#146;s efforts in MEMS. Six&nbsp;years later, he became Director of the MEMS Business Unit, responsible for design,
manufacturing and marketing of ST&#146;s MEMS accelerometers and gyroscopes. These have been successfully adopted by large consumer equipment manufacturers for <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">motion-activated</FONT> user interfaces in many popular
devices, including the Nintendo Wii game console and a wide range of successful smartphones and tablets. In 2007, Mr.&nbsp;Vigna&#146;s scope was enlarged to include management of Sensors, RF, <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">High-Performance</FONT>
Analog and Mixed Signal, Interface, Audio for Portable, and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">General-Purpose</FONT> Analog products. Mr.&nbsp;Vigna has filed more than 150&nbsp;patents on micromachining to date, authored numerous publications in
this field, and delivered many invited speeches at international conferences. He has served as industrial consultant for the President of the Italian Scientific Research Center and sits on the industrial board of several <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">EU-funded</FONT> programs. Mr.&nbsp;Vigna graduated with a degree in Subnuclear Physics from the University of Pisa, Italy. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As is common in the semiconductor industry, our success depends to a significant extent upon, among other factors, the continued service of our key senior executives and research and development,
engineering, marketing, sales, manufacturing, support and other personnel, and on our ability to continue to attract, retain and motivate qualified personnel. The competition for such employees is intense, and the loss of the services of any of
these key personnel without adequate replacement or the inability to attract new qualified personnel could have a material adverse effect on us. We do not maintain insurance with respect to the loss of any of our key personnel. See
&#147;Item&nbsp;3. Key Information &#151; Risk Factors &#151; Risks Related to Our Operations &#151; Loss of key employees could hurt our competitive position&#148;. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The total amount paid as compensation in 2013 to our senior management on
duty on December&nbsp;31, 2013, including Mr.&nbsp;Carlo Bozotti, the sole member of our Managing Board and our President and CEO, was approximately $17.4&nbsp;million before any withholding taxes. Such amount also includes the amounts of EIP paid
to the senior management pursuant to a Corporate Executive Incentive Program (the &#147;EIP&#148;) that entitles selected executives to a yearly bonus based upon the individual performance of such executives. The maximum bonus awarded under the EIP
is based upon a percentage of the executive&#146;s salary and is adjusted to reflect our overall performance. The participants in the EIP must satisfy certain personal objectives that are focused, inter alia, on return on net assets, customer
service, profit, cash flow and market share. The relative charges and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-cash</FONT> benefits were approximately $10.9&nbsp;million. Within such amount, the remuneration of the current sole member of our Managing
Board and President and CEO in 2013 was: </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:12px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Including stock awards, employer social contributions, company car allowance, pension contributions and miscellaneous allowances. In accordance with
the resolutions adopted at our Annual General Meeting of Shareholders held on May&nbsp;30, 2012, the bonus of the sole member of our Managing Board and our President and CEO during the 2013 financial year included a portion of a bonus payable in
stock awards and corresponding to 33,621&nbsp;shares based on fulfillment of a number of <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">pre-defined</FONT> objectives. </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti was <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">re-appointed</FONT> as sole member of our Managing Board and
President and Chief Executive Officer of our company by our Annual General Meeting of Shareholders on May&nbsp;3, 2011 for a three&nbsp;year period. In each of the years 2010, 2011 and 2012, Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti was granted, in accordance with the
compensation policy approved by our Shareholders&#146; meeting, up to 100,000 unvested Stock Awards. The vesting of such stock awards is conditional upon certain performance criteria, fixed by our Supervisory Board, being achieved as well as
Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti&#146;s continued service with us. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In 2009, our Supervisory Board approved the terms of
Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti&#146;s employment by us, which are consistent with the compensation policy approved by our 2005 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Effective May&nbsp;1, 2011, the terms of Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti&#146;s employment were further modified and reviewed by our Supervisory Board. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti has two&nbsp;employment agreements with us, the first with our Dutch parent company, which relates to
his activities as sole member of our Managing Board and representative of the Dutch legal entity, and the second in Switzerland, which relates to his activities as President and CEO, EIP, Pension and other items covered by the compensation policy
approved by our shareholders. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As of January&nbsp;1, 2013, the relationship between a member of the managing
board and a listed Dutch company can no longer be treated as an employment agreement. In practice, it will be treated as a mandate agreement. However, existing employment agreements, including the employment agreement between us and our sole member
of the Managing Board, will remain in effect. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Consistent with this compensation policy, the Supervisory
Board, upon the recommendation of its compensation committee, set the criteria to be met for Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti for attribution of his 2013 bonus (based on new product introductions, market share and budget targets, as well as corporate governance
initiatives). The Supervisory Board, however, has not yet determined the amount of the CEO bonus for 2013. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">With regard to Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti&#146;s 2010 stock awards, the Supervisory Board, upon recommendation of the Compensation
Committee, set the criteria for the attribution of the 100,000 stock awards permitted. The Supervisory Board noted that only two&nbsp;out of the three&nbsp;performance criteria linked to sales, operating income and cash flow had been met under the
employee stock award plan and concluded that Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti was entitled to 66,672 stock awards, which vest as defined by the plan one&nbsp;year, two&nbsp;years and three&nbsp;years, respectively, after the date of the grant provided
Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti is still an employee at such time (subject to the acceleration provisions in the event of a change in control). </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">89 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">With regard to Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti&#146;s 2011 stock awards, the Supervisory
Board, upon recommendation of the Compensation Committee, noted that none of the three&nbsp;performance criteria linked to sales, operating income and return on net assets had been met under the employee stock award plan and concluded that
Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti was not entitled to any stock award. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">With regard to Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti&#146;s 2012 stock
awards, the Supervisory Board, upon recommendation of the Compensation Committee, noted that only two&nbsp;out of the three&nbsp;performance criteria linked to sales, operating income and cash flow had been met under the employee stock award plan
and concluded that Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti was entitled to 66,672 stock awards, which vest as defined by the plan one&nbsp;year, two&nbsp;years and three&nbsp;years, respectively, after the date of the grant provided Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti is still an employee
at such time (subject to the acceleration provisions in the event of a change in control). </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">During 2013,
Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti did not exercise any stock options granted to him, and did not sell any vested stock awards or purchase or sell any of our shares. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">With regard to Mr.&nbsp;Bozotti&#146;s 2013 stock awards, the Supervisory Board, upon recommendation of the Compensation Committee, set the criteria for the attribution of the 100,000 stock awards
permitted. The Supervisory Board, however, has not yet determined whether the performance criteria which condition the vesting (and which, as for all executive employees benefiting from unvested share awards, are linked to sales, operating income
and cash flow) have been met. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our Supervisory Board has approved the establishment of a complementary pension
plan for our top executive management, comprising the CEO, and other key executives to be selected by the CEO, according to the general criteria of eligibility and service set up by the Supervisory Board upon the proposal of its Compensation
Committee. With respect to such plan, we have set up an independent foundation under Swiss law which manages the plan and to which we make contributions. Pursuant to this plan, in 2013 we made a contribution of $0.3&nbsp;million to the plan of our
current President and Chief Executive Officer and $0.6&nbsp;million to the plan for all other beneficiaries. The amount of pension plan payments made for other beneficiaries, such as former employees retired in 2013 and no longer salaried in 2013,
was $0.8&nbsp;million. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We did not extend any loans or overdrafts to our Supervisory Board members or to the
sole member of our Managing Board and President and CEO. Furthermore, we have not guaranteed any debts or concluded any leases with our Supervisory Board members or their families, or the sole member of the Managing Board or his family. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">For information regarding stock options and other stock based compensation granted to members of our Supervisory Board,
the Managing Board and our senior management, please refer to &#147;&#151; Stock Awards and Options&#148; below. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The current members of our Executive Committee and the Managing Board were covered in 2013&nbsp;under certain group life and medical insurance programs provided by us. The aggregate additional amount set
aside by us in 2013 to provide pension, retirement or similar benefits for our Executive Committee and our Managing Board as a group is in addition to the amounts allocated to the complementary pension plan described above and is estimated to have
been approximately $5.2&nbsp;million, which includes statutory employer contributions for state run retirement, similar benefit programs and other miscellaneous allowances. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Share Ownership </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">None of the members of our Supervisory
Board and Managing Board or our senior management holds shares or options to acquire shares representing more than 1% of our issued share capital. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Stock Awards and Options </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our stock-based compensation
plans are designed to incentivize, attract and retain our executives and key employees by aligning compensation with our performance and the evolution of our share price. We have adopted stock based compensation plans comprising either stock options
or unvested stock awards for our senior management as well as key employees. Upon the proposal of our Supervisory Board, our Annual General Meeting of Shareholders held on June&nbsp;21, 2013 resolved to abolish and terminate the <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">stock-based</FONT> compensation for members and professionals of our Supervisory Board as (previously) included in the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">three-year</FONT> <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">stock-based</FONT>
compensation plans for members and professionals of the Supervisory Board. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">90 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Pursuant to the shareholders&#146; resolutions adopted by our general
meetings of shareholders, our Supervisory Board, upon the proposal of the Managing Board and the recommendation of the Compensation Committee, took the following actions: </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="5%"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="justify"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">&#149;</FONT></P></TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">approved, for a five&nbsp;year period, our 2008 unvested Stock Award Plan for Executives and Key Employees, under which directors, managers and
selected employees may be granted stock awards upon the fulfillment of restricted criteria, such as those linked to our performance and continued service with us; </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
