<TD WIDTH="5%"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="justify"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">&#149;</FONT></P></TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">approved conditions relating to our 2009 unvested stock award allocation under the 2008&nbsp;Stock Award Plan, including restriction criteria linked
to our performance; </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="justify"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">&#149;</FONT></P></TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">approved conditions relating to our 2010 unvested stock award allocation under the 2008&nbsp;Stock Award Plan, including restriction criteria linked
to our performance; </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">approved conditions relating to our 2011 unvested stock award allocation under the 2008&nbsp;Stock Award Plan, including restriction criteria linked
to our performance; and </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

<TD WIDTH="5%"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="justify"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">&#149;</FONT></P></TD>
<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">approved conditions relating to our 2012 unvested stock award allocation under the 2008&nbsp;Stock Award Plan, including restriction criteria linked
to our performance. </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">At our Annual General Meeting of Shareholders held on June&nbsp;21,
2013, it was resolved to approve a new <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">four-year</FONT> Unvested Stock Award Plan for the Management and Key Employees, which provides that stock awards may be granted under restricted criteria to selected employees.
</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We use our treasury shares to cover the stock awards granted under the unvested stock award plans. In the
year ended as of December&nbsp;31, 2013, 2,507,616 stock awards granted in relation to 2010 and 2012 had vested, leaving 20,096,542 treasury shares outstanding. The stock award allocation for 2013 generated an additional charge of $13&nbsp;million
in the consolidated statement of income for 2013, which corresponds to the cost per service in the year for all granted shares that are (or are expected to be) vested pursuant to the financial performance criteria being met. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The exercise of stock options and the sale or purchase of shares of our stock by the members or professionals of our
Supervisory Board, the sole member of our Managing Board and President and CEO, and all our employees are subject to an internal policy which involves, inter alia, certain blackout periods. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>Employee and Managing Board Stock Based Compensation Plans </I></B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>2001 Stock Option Plan</I>. Our 2001 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders approved resolutions authorizing the
Supervisory Board, for a period of five&nbsp;years, to adopt and administer a stock option plan (in the form of five annual tranches) that provided for the granting to our managers and professionals of options to purchase up to a maximum of
60&nbsp;million common shares (the &#147;2001 Stock Option Plan&#148;). The amount of options granted to the sole member of our Managing Board and President and CEO is determined by our Compensation Committee, upon delegation from our Supervisory
Board and, since 2005, has been submitted for approval by our annual shareholders&#146; meeting. The amount of stock options granted to other employees was made by our Compensation Committee on delegation by our Supervisory Board and following the
recommendation of the sole member of our Managing Board and President and CEO. In addition, the Supervisory Board delegated to the sole member of our Managing Board and President and CEO the flexibility to grant, each year, up to a determined number
of share awards to our employees pursuant to the 2001 Stock Option Plan in special cases or in connection with an acquisition. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In 2005, our shareholders at our Annual General Meeting of Shareholders approved a modification to our 2001&nbsp;Stock Option Plan so as to provide the grant of up to four&nbsp;million unvested stock
awards instead of stock options to our senior executives and certain of our key employees, as well as the grant of up to 100,000 unvested stock awards instead of stock options to our President and CEO. A total of 4,159,915&nbsp;unvested stock awards
have been granted pursuant to the modification of such plan, which includes unvested stock awards that were granted to employees who subsequently left our Company thereby forfeiting their awards. Certain forfeited unvested stock awards were
subsequently awarded to other employees. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Pursuant to such approval, the Compensation Committee, upon
delegation from our Supervisory Board, approved the conditions that apply to the vesting of such awards. These conditions related to both our financial </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">91 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">
performance, pursuant to certain defined criteria in 2005 and during the first quarter of 2006, and the continued presence of the beneficiaries of the unvested stock awards at the defined vesting
dates in 2006, 2007 and 2008. Of the shares awarded, none remain outstanding and unvested as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. </FONT></P>

</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>2008 Unvested Share Award Plan &#151; 2010 Allocation </I></B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In accordance with the Employee Unvested Share Award Plan, as approved by our 2008 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders
and further approved by our 2010 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders, up to 6,516,460 unvested stock awards could be granted to our senior executives and certain of our key employees. Our shareholders at our annual shareholders&#146; meeting in
2010&nbsp;approved the grant of up to 100,000 unvested stock awards to our President and CEO. 6,566,375&nbsp;unvested stock awards have been granted under such allocation as of December&nbsp;31, 2013 out of which none remain outstanding and unvested
as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>2008 Unvested Share Award Plan &#151; 2011 Allocation </I></B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In accordance with the Employee Unvested Share Award Plan, as approved by our 2008 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders
and further approved by our 2011 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders, up to 6,150,000 unvested stock awards could be granted to our senior executives and certain of our key employees. Our shareholders at our annual shareholders&#146; meeting in
2011&nbsp;approved the grant of up to 100,000 unvested stock awards to our President and CEO. 5,976,630&nbsp;unvested stock awards have been granted under such allocation as of December&nbsp;31, 2013, out of which none remain outstanding and
unvested as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>2008 Unvested Share Award Plan &#151; 2012 Allocation </I></B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In accordance with the Employee Unvested Share Award Plan, as approved by our 2008 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders
and further approved by our 2012 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders, up to 6,500,000 unvested stock awards could be granted to our senior executives and certain of our key employees. Our shareholders at our annual shareholders&#146; meeting in
2012 approved the grant of up to 100,000 unvested Stock Awards to our President and CEO. 6,520,765&nbsp;unvested stock awards have been granted under such allocation as of December&nbsp;31, 2013, out of which 3,152,539 remain outstanding but
unvested as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>2013 Unvested Share Award Plan &#151; 2013 Allocation </I></B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In accordance with the Employee Unvested Share Award Plan, as approved by our 2013 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders,
up to 6,900,000 unvested stock awards could be granted to our senior executives and certain of our key employees. Our shareholders at our annual shareholders&#146; meeting in 2013 approved the grant of up to 100,000 unvested stock awards to our
President and CEO. 6,412,045&nbsp;unvested stock awards have been granted under such allocation as of December&nbsp;31, 2013, out of which 6,379,320 remain outstanding but unvested as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. </FONT></P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Pursuant to such approval, the Compensation Committee, upon delegation from
our Supervisory Board, has approved the conditions which shall apply (when applicable) to the vesting of such awards. These conditions relate both to our financial performance meeting certain defined criteria in 2013, and to the continued presence
at the defined vesting dates in 2014, 2015 and 2016 of the beneficiaries of the unvested stock awards. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Furthermore, the Compensation Committee, on behalf of the entire Supervisory Board and with the approval of the entire
Supervisory Board, approved the list of beneficiaries of the unvested stock awards and delegated to our President and Chief Executive Officer the right to grant certain additional unvested stock awards to key employees, in exceptional cases,
provided that the total number of unvested stock awards granted to executives and key employees shall not exceed 6,900,000 for 2013. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>Supervisory Board Stock Based Compensation Plans </I></B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>2002 Stock Option Plan for members and professionals of our Supervisory Board</I>. Our 2002 Annual General Meeting of
Shareholders approved the adoption of a stock option plan for members and professionals of our Supervisory Board (the &#147;2002 Stock Option Plan&#148;). The 2002 Stock Option Plan provided for the grant of 12,000 options per year to each member of
our Supervisory Board and 6,000 options per year to the professionals of our Supervisory Board. Pursuant to the 2002 Stock Option Plan, stock options for the subscription of 396,000&nbsp;shares were granted to the members and professionals of our
Supervisory Board, as shown in the table below: </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>2002 Stock Option Plan </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>(for Supervisory Board members and professionals) </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:12px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">At December&nbsp;31, 2013, options to purchase a total of 132,000 common shares were
outstanding under the 2002 Stock Option Plan. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>2005, 2006 and 2007 Stock based Compensation for members and
professionals of the Supervisory Board</I>. Our 2005 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders approved the adoption of a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">three-year</FONT> <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">stock-based</FONT> compensation plan for
Supervisory Board members and professionals. The plan provided for the grant of a maximum number of 6,000 stock awards per year for each member of the Supervisory Board and 3,000&nbsp;stock awards for each of the professionals of the Supervisory
Board at an exercise price of &#128;1.04&nbsp;per share, corresponding to the nominal value of our share. Pursuant to our 2007 annual shareholders&#146; meeting, the 2005&nbsp;plan was modified and the maximum number was increased to 15,000 stock
awards per year for each member of the Supervisory Board and 7,500 stock awards per year for each professional of the Supervisory Board for the remaining year of the plan. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In 2005, 66,000&nbsp;stock awards were granted to the beneficiaries under such plan, which had completely vested as of
December&nbsp;31, 2008. In 2006, 66,000&nbsp;stock awards were granted to the beneficiaries under such plan, which had all vested as of December&nbsp;31, 2009. In 2007, 165,000&nbsp;stock awards were granted to the beneficiaries under such plan,
which had all vested as of December&nbsp;31, 2010. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The table below reflects the grants to the Supervisory
Board members and professionals under the 2005&nbsp;Stock Based Compensation Plan as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:12px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>2008, 2009 and 2010 Stock based Compensation for members and
professionals of the Supervisory Board</I>. Our 2008 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders approved the adoption of a new three&nbsp;year stock based compensation plan for Supervisory Board members and professionals. This plan provides for the
grant of a maximum number of 15,000 stock awards per year for each member of the Supervisory Board and 7,500&nbsp;stock awards for each of the professionals of the Supervisory Board at an exercise price of &#128;1.04&nbsp;per share, corresponding to
the nominal value of our shares. In 2008, 165,000&nbsp;stock awards were granted to the beneficiaries under such plan, out of which 75,000 were outstanding as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. In 2009, 165,000&nbsp;stock awards were granted to the
beneficiaries under such plan, out of which 75,000 were outstanding as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. In 2010, 172,500&nbsp;stock awards were granted to the beneficiaries under such plan, out of which 82,500 were outstanding as of December&nbsp;31,
2013. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The table below reflects the grants to the Supervisory Board members and professionals under the
2008&nbsp;Stock Based Compensation Plan as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:12px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD></TR>
</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>2011 and 2012 <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Stock-based</FONT> Compensation for
members and professionals of the Supervisory Board</I>. Our 2011 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders approved the adoption of a new three&nbsp;year stock based compensation plan for Supervisory Board members and professionals. This plan provides
for the grant of a maximum number of 15,000 stock awards per&nbsp;year for each member of the Supervisory Board and 7,500 stock awards for each of the professionals of the Supervisory Board at an exercise price of &#128;1.04&nbsp;per share,
corresponding to the nominal value of our shares. In 2011, 172,500&nbsp;stock awards were granted to the beneficiaries under such plan, out of which 117,500 were outstanding as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. In 2012, 180,000&nbsp;stock awards were
granted to the beneficiaries under such plan, out of which 122,500 were outstanding as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">At our Annual General Meeting of Shareholders held on June&nbsp;21, 2013, it was resolved to abolish and terminate the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">stock-based</FONT> compensation for our Supervisory
Board members and professionals as (previously) included in the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">three-year</FONT> <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Stock-Based</FONT> Compensation Plan for members and professionals of the Supervisory Board. No
options were granted in 2013 to the Supervisory Board. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:12px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our future success, particularly in a period of strong increased demand,
will partly depend on our ability to continue to attract, retain and motivate highly qualified technical, marketing, engineering and management personnel. Unions are represented at several of our manufacturing facilities. We use temporary employees,
if required, during production spikes and, in Europe, during summer vacations. We have not experienced any significant strikes or work stoppages in recent years. Management believes that our relations with employees are good. </FONT></P>

</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our principal shareholders do not have different voting rights from those of our other
shareholders. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, FT1CI (the &#147;French Shareholder&#148;), which is controlled
by Bpifrance and CEA, and the Italian Ministry of the Economy and Finance (the &#147;Italian Shareholder&#148;), each directly held 50% in STMicroelectronics Holding N.V. (&#147;ST Holding&#148;). The indirect interest of the French Shareholder and
the Italian Shareholder in us is split on a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">50%-50%&nbsp;basis.</FONT> Through a structured tracking stock system implemented in the Articles of Association of ST Holding, the French Shareholder and the Italian
Shareholder each indirectly hold 125,352,377 of our common shares, representing approximately 13.7% of our issued share capital as of December&nbsp;31, 2013. Any disposals or, as the case may be, acquisitions by ST Holding on behalf of the French
Shareholder or the Italian Shareholder, will decrease or, as the case may be, increase the indirect interest of, respectively, the French Shareholder or the Italian Shareholder, in our issued share capital. FT1CI is a jointly held company
established to control the interest of French shareholders in ST Holding. As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, Bpifrance (as defined below) and CEA are the sole shareholders of FT1CI, holding respectively 79.2% and 20.8% of FT1CI&#146;s share capital.
Bpifrance is an investment fund 50% owned by Caisse des d&eacute;p&ocirc;ts et consignations and 50% owned by the French State. On July&nbsp;12, 2013, all of the shares of Fonds Strat&eacute;gique d&#146;Investissement (&#147;FSI&#148;) were
transferred to BPI Groupe SA, a company co-controlled on a 50/50 basis by the French State (through the Etablissement Public Industriel et Commercial BPI Groupe (&#147;EPIC BPI Groupe&#148;)) and Caisse des D&eacute;p&ocirc;ts et Consignations, and
FSI was renamed Bpifrance Participations (&#147;Bpifrance&#148;). This reorganization does not affect the shareholding in the Company. CEA is a French government funded technological research organization. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">STMicroelectronics Holding II B.V. (&#147;ST Holding II&#148;) owned 90% of our shares before our initial public offering
in 1994, and over time, gradually reduced its participation, going below the 66% threshold in 1997 and below the 50% threshold in 1999. ST Holding II merged with ST Holding, effective June&nbsp;1, 2012 and ceased to exist. ST Holding may dispose of
our shares as provided below in &#147;&#151; STH Shareholders&#146; Agreement &#151;Disposals of our Common Shares&#148;. Set forth below is a table of ST Holding&#146;s holdings (as successor to ST Holding II) in us as of the end of 2013 and 2012
and ST Holding II&#146;s holdings as of the end of 2011: </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:12px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The chart below illustrates the shareholding structure as of
December&nbsp;31, 2013: </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:12px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center">


<IMG SRC="g622882g04o80.jpg" ALT="LOGO">
 </P> <P STYLE="line-height:8px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:2px;border-bottom:0.5pt solid #000000;width:10%">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="4%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="justify"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">(1)</FONT></P></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In addition to the 27.5% held by ST Holding and the 70.3% held by the Public, 2.2% are held by us as Treasury Shares. </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>Shareholders&#146; Agreement </I></B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>STH Shareholders&#146; Agreement </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We were formed in 1987 as
a result of the decision by Thomson CSF (now called Thales) and STET (now called Telecom Italia S.p.A.) to combine their semiconductor businesses and to enter into a shareholders&#146; agreement on April&nbsp;30, 1987, which was amended on
December&nbsp;10, 2001, restated on March&nbsp;17, 2004 and further amended on February&nbsp;26, 2008. The February&nbsp;26, 2008 amended and restated agreement (as amended, the &#147;STH Shareholders&#146; Agreement&#148;) supersedes and replaces
all previous agreements. The current parties to the STH Shareholders&#146; Agreement are Bpifrance, CEA and their joint company FT1CI (the &#147;French Shareholder&#148;) and the Ministry of the Economy and Finance (the &#147;Italian
Shareholder&#148;). </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Pursuant to the terms of the STH Shareholders&#146; Agreement, the parties have agreed to
certain corporate governance rights provided that they maintain certain levels of respective interests in ST Holding and in the Company&#146;s share capital. See further details below. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Merger of the Holding Companies </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The French Shareholder and the Italian Shareholder merged the two&nbsp;holding companies (ST Holding and ST Holding II), effective June&nbsp;1, 2012, in order to simplify the structure through which they
own their interests in us. ST Holding II ceased to exist, while ST Holding continues to hold our common shares. The company that now holds or may hold our common shares in the future for indirect shareholders is referred to below as the
&#147;holding company&#148;. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Standstill </I></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The STH Shareholders&#146; Agreement contains a standstill provision that precludes any of the parties and the
parties&#146; affiliates from acquiring, directly or indirectly, any of our common shares or any instrument providing for the right to acquire any of our common shares other than through the holding company. The standstill is in effect for as long
as such party holds our common shares through the holding company. The parties agreed to continue to hold their stakes in us at all times through the current holding structure of the holding company, subject to certain limited exceptions.
</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Corporate Governance </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The STH Shareholders&#146; Agreement provides for a balanced corporate governance between FT1CI and the Ministry of the Economy and Finance (FT1CI and the Ministry of the Economy and Finance are
collectively </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">96 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">
defined as &#147;STH Shareholders&#148; and individually defined as &#147;STH Shareholder&#148;) for the duration of the &#147;Balance Period&#148;, despite actual differences in indirect
economic interest in us. The &#147;Balance Period&#148; lasts as long as each STH Shareholder owns at any time a voting stake in ST Holding equal to at least 47.5% of the total voting stakes of ST Holding. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As of January&nbsp;1, 2012, if any STH Shareholder falls under this threshold, it will not be able to restore the Balance
Period by subsequently increasing its voting stake, and the Balance Period will terminate, unless the parties agree otherwise. The STH Shareholders&#146; Agreement provides that during the Balance Period, ST Holding will have a managing board
comprised of two&nbsp;members (one member designated by FT1CI, and one&nbsp;designated by the Ministry of the Economy and Finance) and a supervisory board comprised of six members (three&nbsp;designated by FT1CI and three&nbsp;designated by the
Ministry of the Economy and Finance). The chairman of the supervisory board of the holding company shall be designated for a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">three-year</FONT> term by one&nbsp;shareholder (with the other shareholder entitled to
designate the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Vice-Chairman),</FONT> such designation to alternate between the Ministry of the Economy and Finance on the one hand and FT1CI on the other hand. The current Chairman of the Supervisory Board of ST
Holding is Mr.&nbsp;Alain Dutheil and the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Vice-Chairman</FONT> is Mr.&nbsp;Luciano Acciari. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As regards STMicroelectronics N.V., the STH Shareholders&#146; Agreement provides that during the Balance Period: (i)&nbsp;each of the STH Shareholders (FT1CI, on the one hand, and the Ministry of the
Economy and Finance, on the other hand) shall have the right to insert on a list prepared for proposal by the holding company to our annual shareholders meeting the same number of members for election to the Supervisory Board, and the holding
company shall vote in favor of such members; (ii)&nbsp;the STH Shareholders will cause the holding company to submit to our annual shareholders meeting and to vote in favor of a common proposal for the appointment of the Managing Board; and
(iii)&nbsp;any decision relating to the voting rights of the holding company in us shall require the unanimous approval of the holding company shareholders and shall be submitted by the holding company to our annual shareholders meeting. The STH
Shareholders Agreement also provides that the Chairman of our Supervisory Board will be designated upon proposal of an STH Shareholder for a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">three-year</FONT> term, and the
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Vice-Chairman</FONT> of our Supervisory Board will be designated upon proposal of the other STH Shareholder for the same period, and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">vice-versa</FONT> for the following <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">three-year</FONT> term. The STH Shareholders further agreed that the STH Shareholder proposing the appointment of the Chairman be entitled to propose the appointment of the Assistant Secretary of our Supervisory Board, and
the STH Shareholder proposing the appointment of the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Vice-Chairman</FONT> be entitled to propose the appointment of the Secretary of our Supervisory Board. Finally, each STH Shareholder is entitled to appoint a
Financial Controller to the Supervisory Board. Our Secretary, Assistant Secretary and two&nbsp;Financial Controllers are referred to as professionals (not members) of our Supervisory Board. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">During the Balance Period, any other decision, to the extent that a resolution of the holding company is required, must
be pursuant to the unanimous approval of the shareholders, including but not limited to the following: (i)&nbsp;the definition of the role and structure of our Managing Board and Supervisory Board, and those of the holding company; (ii)&nbsp;the
powers of the Chairman and the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Vice-Chairman</FONT> of our Supervisory Board, and that of the holding company; (iii)&nbsp;information by the holding company&#146;s managing board and supervisory board, and those of
us; (iv)&nbsp;treatment of confidential information; (v)&nbsp;appointment of any additional members of our Managing Board and that of the holding company; (vi)&nbsp;remuneration of the members of our Managing Board and those of the holding company;
(vii)&nbsp;internal audit of STMicroelectronics N.V. and of the holding company; (viii)&nbsp;industrial and commercial relationships between STMicroelectronics N.V. and the Ministry of the Economy and Finance or STMicroelectronics N.V. and either or
both FT1CI shareholders, or any of their affiliates; and (ix)&nbsp;any of the decisions listed in article&nbsp;16.1 of our Articles of Association including our budget and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">pluri-annual</FONT> plans. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In addition, the following resolutions, to the extent that a resolution of the holding company is required, must be
resolved upon by a shareholders&#146; resolution of the holding company, which shall require the unanimous approval of the STH Shareholders: (i)&nbsp;any alteration in the holding company&#146;s articles of association; (ii)&nbsp;any issue,
acquisition or disposal by the holding company of its shares or change in share rights; (iii)&nbsp;any alteration in our authorized share capital or issue by us of new shares and/or of any financial instrument giving rights to subscribe for our
common shares; any acquisition or disposal by the holding company of our shares and/or any right to subscribe for our common shares; any modification to the rights attached to our common shares; any merger, acquisition or joint venture agreement to
which we are or are proposed to be a party; and any other items on the agenda of our general shareholders&#146; meeting; (iv)&nbsp;the liquidation or dissolution of the holding company; (v)&nbsp;any legal merger, legal
<FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">de-merger,</FONT> acquisition or joint venture agreement to which the holding company is proposed to be a party; and (vi)&nbsp;the adoption or approval of our annual accounts or those of the holding company or a
resolution concerning a dividend distribution by us. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">97 </FONT></P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">At the end of the Balance Period (i.e., once a shareholder&#146;s voting
stake in ST Holding has decreased under the 47.5% threshold (such STH Shareholder being thereafter referred to as &#147;minority shareholder&#148; and the other one being referred to as &#147;majority shareholder&#148;)), the members of our
Supervisory Board and those of the holding company designated by the minority shareholder of the holding company will immediately resign upon request of the holding company&#146;s majority shareholder, subject to the rights described in the
following paragraph. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">After the end of the Balance Period, unanimous approval by the shareholders of the
holding company remains required to approve: </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

Shareholders&#146; aggregate stake in us at such time, with respect to the holding company, any changes to the articles of association, any issue, acquisition or disposal of shares in the holding company or change in the rights of its shares, its

purposes of a quorum of shareholders at one of our shareholders meetings, but will not be voted (i.e., will be abstained from the vote in a way that they will not be counted as a negative vote or as a positive vote). </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE>

<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In addition, the minority shareholder will have the right to designate at least one member of the list of candidates for our Supervisory Board to be
proposed by the holding company if that shareholder indirectly owns at least 3% of our total issued and outstanding share capital, with the majority STH Shareholder retaining the right to appoint that number of members to our Supervisory Board that
is at least proportional to such majority shareholder&#146;s voting stake. </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Finally, at the
end of the Balance Period, the unanimous approval required for other decisions taken at the STMicroelectronics N.V. level shall only be compulsory to the extent possible, taking into account the actual power attached to the direct and indirect
shareholding together held by the STH Shareholders in our company. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Disposals of our Common Shares </I></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The STH Shareholders&#146; Agreement provides that each STH Shareholder retains the right to cause the holding company to
dispose of its stake in us at its sole discretion, provided it is pursuant to either (i)&nbsp;the issuance of financial instruments, (ii)&nbsp;an equity swap, (iii)&nbsp;a structured finance deal or (iv)&nbsp;a straight sale. The holding company may
enter into escrow arrangements with STH Shareholders with respect to our shares, whether this be pursuant to exchangeable notes, securities lending or other financial instruments. STH&nbsp;Shareholders that dispose of our shares through the issuance
of exchangeable instruments, an equity swap or a structured finance deal maintain the voting rights of the underlying shares in their ST Holding voting stake provided that such rights remain freely and continuously held by the holding company as
though the holding company were still holding the full ownership of the shares. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As long as any of the parties
to the STH Shareholders&#146; Agreement has a direct or indirect interest in us, except in the case of a public offer, no sales by a party may be made of any of our shares or of FT1CI, ST&nbsp;Holding or to any of our top ten competitors, or any
company that controls such competitor. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Change of Control Provision </I></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The STH Shareholders&#146; Agreement provides for <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">tag-along</FONT> rights, preemptive
rights, and provisions with respect to a change of control of any of the shareholders or any controlling shareholder of FT1CI, on the one </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">98 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">
hand, and the Ministry of the Economy and Finance, on the other hand. The shareholders may transfer shares of the holding company or FT1CI to any of the shareholders&#146; affiliates, which would
include the Italian state or the French state with respect to entities controlled by a state. The shareholders and their ultimate shareholders will be prohibited from launching any takeover process on any of the other shareholders. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Deadlock </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In the event of a disagreement that cannot be resolved between the parties as to the conduct of the business and actions contemplated by the STH Shareholders&#146; Agreement, each party has the right to
offer its interest in ST Holding to the other, which then has the right to acquire, or to have a third party acquire, such interest. If neither party agrees to acquire or have acquired the other party&#146;s interest, then together the parties are
obligated to try to find a third party to acquire their collective interests, or such part thereof as is suitable to resolve the deadlock. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Duration </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The STH Shareholders&#146; Agreement will remain
in force as long as the Ministry of the Economy and Finance, on the one&nbsp;hand, and any of Bpifrance, FT1CI or CEA, on the other hand, are shareholders of the holding company. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Statutory Considerations </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As is the case with other companies controlled by the French government, the French government may appoint a Commissaire du Gouvernement and a Contr&ocirc;leur d&#146;Etat for FT1CI. Pursuant to Decree <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">No.&nbsp;94-214,</FONT> dated March&nbsp;10, 1994, these government representatives have the right (i)&nbsp;to attend any board meeting of FT1CI, and (ii)&nbsp;to veto any board resolution or any decision of the president
of FT1CI within ten&nbsp;days of such board meeting (or, if they have not attended the meeting, within ten&nbsp;days of the receipt of the board minutes or the notification of such president&#146;s decision); such veto lapses if not confirmed within
one&nbsp;month by the Ministry of the Economy or the Ministry of the Industry. FT1CI is subject to certain points of the Decree of August&nbsp;9, 1953 pursuant to which the Ministry of the Economy and any other relevant ministries have the authority
to approve decisions of FT1CI relating to budgets or forecasts of revenues, operating expenses and capital expenditures. The effect of these provisions may be that the decisions taken by us and our subsidiaries that, by the terms of the STH
Shareholders&#146; Agreement, require prior approval by FT1CI, may be adversely affected by these veto rights under French law. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Preference Shares </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On November&nbsp;22, 2006, our Supervisory Board decided to authorize us to enter into an option agreement with an independent foundation, Stichting Continu&iuml;teit ST (the &#147;Stichting&#148;). This
is a common practice used by a majority of publicly traded Dutch companies. Our Managing Board and our Supervisory Board, along with the board of the Stichting, have declared that they are jointly of the opinion that the Stichting is legally
independent of our Company and our major shareholders. Our Supervisory Board approved this option agreement, entered into on January&nbsp;22, 2007, with a duration of ten&nbsp;years, to reflect changes in Dutch legal requirements, not in response to
any hostile takeover attempt. It provides for the issuance of up to a maximum of 540,000,000 preference shares. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Stichting would have the option, which it shall exercise in its sole discretion, to take up the preference shares.
The preference shares would be issuable in the event of actions considered hostile by our Managing Board and Supervisory Board, such as a creeping acquisition (in such case up to 30%&nbsp;minus one&nbsp;share of our issued and outstanding share
capital) or an offer on our common shares, which are unsupported by our Managing Board and Supervisory Board and which the board of the Stichting determines would be contrary to the interests of our Company, our shareholders and our other
stakeholders. If the Stichting exercises its call option and acquires preference shares, it must pay at least 25% of the par value of such preference shares. The preference shares may remain outstanding for no longer than two&nbsp;years. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">No preference shares have been issued to date. The effect of the preference shares may be to deter potential acquirers
from effecting an unsolicited acquisition resulting in a change of control as well as to create a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">level-playing</FONT> field in the event actions which are considered hostile by our Managing Board and Supervisory
Board, as described above, occur and which the board of the Stichting determines to be contrary to our interests and our shareholders and other stakeholders. In addition, any issuance of additional capital within the limits of our authorized share
capital, as approved by our shareholders, is subject to approval by our Supervisory Board, other than pursuant to an exercise of the call option granted to the Stichting. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">99 </FONT></P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Related Party Transactions </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">One of the members of our Supervisory Board is a member of the Board of Directors of Technicolor and one of the members of
our Supervisory Board is a member of the Supervisory Board of BESI. A former member of our Supervisory Board, whose mandate ended in June&nbsp;2013, is a director of Oracle Corporation (&#147;Oracle&#148;) and Flextronics International. A former
member of our senior management, who resigned effective March&nbsp;31, 2013, is a member of the Board of Directors of Soitec and Adecco. Adecco, as well as Oracle&#146;s subsidiary PeopleSoft, supply certain services to our Company. We have also
conducted transactions with Soitec and BESI as well as with Technicolor and Flextronics. From time to time, we may enter into transactions with or invest in subsidiaries of certain of our significant shareholders, former shareholders or other
companies in which they invest (including but not limited to: Adecco, Areva, Altis, MicroOLED and Orange). Each of the aforementioned arrangements and transactions is negotiated without the personal involvement of our Supervisory Board members or,
where applicable, the senior managers concerned, and we believe that they are made in line with market practices and conditions. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">See Note 24 to Our Consolidated Financial Statements for transactions with significant shareholders, their affiliates and other related parties, which also include transactions between us and our <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">equity-method</FONT> investments. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:18px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="8%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="justify"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><A NAME="tx622882_9"></A>Item&nbsp;8.</B></FONT></P></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Financial Information </B></FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Financial Statements </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Please see &#147;Item&nbsp;18.
Financial Statements&#148; for a list of the financial statements filed with this <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Form&nbsp;20-F.</FONT> </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Legal Proceedings </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As is the case with many companies in
the semiconductor industry, we have from time to time received, and may in the future receive, communications from other semiconductor companies or third parties alleging possible infringement of third party patents or other third party intellectual
property rights. In addition, from time to time, we enter into discussions regarding broad patent <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">cross-license</FONT> arrangements with other industry participants and there can be no assurance that such discussions
will be brought to a successful conclusion and result in the intended agreement. Furthermore, we may become involved in costly litigation brought against us regarding patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets or mask works. In the event that
the outcome of such IP litigation would be unfavorable to us, we may be required to take a license for third party patents or other IP rights upon economically unfavorable terms and conditions, and possibly pay damages for prior use, and/or face an
injunction, all of which singly or in the aggregate could have a material adverse effect on our results of operations and ability to compete. See &#147;Item&nbsp;3. Key Information &#151; Risk Factors &#151; Risks Related to Our Operations &#151; We
depend on patents to protect our rights to our technology and may face claims of infringing the IP rights of others&#148; included in this <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">Form&nbsp;20-F,</FONT> which may be updated from time to time in our public
filings. We are also party to certain disputes which are not related to patents or other IP rights. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We record
a provision when, based on our best estimate, we consider it probable that a liability has been incurred and when the amount of the probable loss can be reasonably estimated. As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, provisions for estimated probable losses
with respect to legal proceedings were not considered material. We regularly evaluate losses and claims to determine whether they need to be adjusted based on the most current information available to us and using our best judgment. There can be no
assurance that our recorded reserves will be sufficient to cover the extent of our potential liabilities. Legal costs associated with claims are expensed as incurred. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>We are a party to legal proceedings with Tessera, Inc. (&#147;Tessera&#148;) </I></B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In 2006, Tessera initiated a patent infringement lawsuit against us and numerous other semiconductor manufacturers in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California. Tessera claims that
our ball grid array packages infringe certain patents owned by Tessera, and that we breached a 1997 license agreement by failing to pay royalties to Tessera on sales of products in certain ball grid array packages. Tessera then filed a complaint in
2007 with the U.S. International Trade Commission in Washington, D.C. (&#147;ITC&#148;) against us and numerous other parties. During the ITC proceedings, the District Court action was stayed. On May&nbsp;20, 2009, the ITC issued a limited exclusion
order as well as a cease and desist order, both of which were terminated when the Tessera patents expired in September 2010. The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit subsequently affirmed the ITC&#146;s decision and on November&nbsp;28,
2011 the U.S. Supreme Court denied the defendants&#146; petition </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">100
</FONT></P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">
for review, and the ITC decision became final. In January 2012, the District Court proceedings were revived in California. The District Court has appointed a special master to advise it on
technical issues. Trial is scheduled for August&nbsp;25, 2014. In May 2013, Tessera served its opening expert&#146;s report on damages which opines that Tessera is entitled to $181 million in damages (including interest) based on our sales of
allegedly infringing products from 2000 through 2010. Our opening expert&#146;s report on damages opines that our damages should be more in the range of $5 million to $8 million if an adverse judgment were to be entered against us. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>We were a party to legal proceedings with Rambus Inc. (&#147;Rambus&#148;) </I></B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On December&nbsp;1, 2010, Rambus filed a complaint with the ITC against us and numerous other parties, asserting that we
engaged in unfair trade practices by importing certain semiconductor chips that include memory controllers and/or certain peripheral interface technologies such as SerDes, PCI Express, SATA and SAS that allegedly infringe certain patents owned by
Rambus. The complaint sought an exclusion order to bar importation into the United States of all accused semiconductor chips that infringe any claim of the asserted patents, as well as products of certain party customers incorporating the same. On
July&nbsp;25, 2012, the ITC elected to terminate the ITC investigation with a finding of no violation of section 337 of the Tariff Act of 1930. On September&nbsp;25, 2012, Rambus filed a notice of appeal with the U.S. Court of Appeals for the
Federal Circuit. Also on December&nbsp;1, 2010, Rambus filed a lawsuit against us and other co-defendants in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California alleging infringement of nineteen Rambus patents. On June&nbsp;17, 2013, we
and Rambus announced a comprehensive settlement and license agreement pursuant to which all pending litigation between the parties was resolved. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><I>We and our subsidiaries are also involved in other legal proceedings, claims and litigation arising in the ordinary course of business. </I></B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">All pending claims and litigation proceedings involve complex questions of fact and law and may require the expenditure of
significant funds and the diversion of other resources to prosecute and defend. The results of legal proceedings are inherently uncertain, and material adverse outcomes are possible, including the risk of an injunction. The resolution of
intellectual property litigation may require us to pay damages for past infringement or to obtain a license under the other party&#146;s intellectual property rights that could require <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">one-time</FONT> license fees or
ongoing royalties, which could adversely impact our product gross margins in future periods, or could prevent us from manufacturing or selling some of our products or limit or restrict the focus of employees involved in such litigation with regard
to the work they normally perform for us. From time to time we may enter into confidential discussions regarding the potential settlement of pending litigation or other proceedings; however, there can be no assurance that any such discussions will
occur or will result in a settlement. The settlement of any pending litigation or other proceeding could require us to incur substantial settlement payments and costs. Furthermore, the settlement of any intellectual property proceeding may require
us to grant a license to certain of our intellectual property rights to the other party under a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">cross-license</FONT> agreement. If any of those events were to occur, our business, financial condition and results of
operations could be materially and adversely affected. In addition, from time to time we are approached by holders of intellectual property to engage in discussions about our obtaining licenses to their intellectual property. We will disclose the
nature of any such discussion if we believe that (i)&nbsp;it is probable an intellectual property holder will assert a claim of infringement, (ii)&nbsp;there is a reasonable possibility the outcome (assuming assertion) will be unfavorable, and
(iii)&nbsp;the resulting liability would be material to our financial condition. We also constantly review the merits of litigation and claims which we are facing and decide to make an accrual when we are able to reasonably determine that it is
probable that a liability has been incurred and the amount of the loss can be reasonably estimated. To date, we have not determined on such basis that any of the litigation or claims which we are facing gives rise to a probable material liability,
singly or in the aggregate. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Risk Management and Insurance </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We cover our industrial and business risks through insurance policies and programs with leading insurance carriers with at
least investment grade ratings, to the extent reasonably permissible by the insurance market which does not provide insurance coverage for certain risks and imposes certain limits, terms and conditions on coverage that it does provide. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Risks may be covered either through local policies or through corporate policies negotiated on a worldwide level for the
ST Group of Companies. Corporate insurance policies and programs are negotiated when the risks are recurrent in several of our affiliated companies. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Currently we have four corporate insurance policies and programs covering the following risks: </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">

<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Transportation risks. </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our insurance policies generally cover a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">twelve-month</FONT> period although may be subscribed for a longer period if conditions for a longer term arrangement are deemed
beneficial to us. Such policies are subject to certain terms and conditions, exclusions and limitations, generally in line with prevailing conditions, exclusions and limitations, in the insurance market. Pursuant to such conditions, risks such as
terrorism, earthquake, fire, floods, consequential damages and loss of production, may not be fully insured and we may not, in the event of a claim under a policy, receive an indemnification from our insurers commensurate with the full amount of the
damage we have incurred. Furthermore, our product liability insurance covers physical and direct damages, which may be caused by our products; however, immaterial, <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">non-consequential</FONT> damages resulting from
failure to deliver or delivery of defective products might not be covered because such risks are considered to occur in the ordinary course of business and might not be insured. We may decide to subscribe for excess coverage in addition to the
coverage provided by our standard policies. If we suffer damage or incur a claim, which is not covered by one of our corporate insurance policies, this may have a material adverse effect on our results of operations. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Risk and fire engineering site assessments are performed, on an annual basis, through our leading property insurer&#146;s
professional fire and risk engineers in the field of property damage and business interruption in our production sites, to assess potential losses and actual risk exposures, including natural catastrophes. Then, such assessments are usually provided
to insurers, <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">co-insurers</FONT> and <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">re-insurers</FONT> underwriters to subscribe our risks. We do not own or operate any insurance captive companies, which acts as insurer for our own
risks, although we may consider such an option in the future. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The company, under the direction of our CEO and
supervision of the Audit Committee of our Supervisory Board, has implemented a corporate Enterprise Risk Management (&#147;ERM&#148;) process, which is led by our Chief Audit and Risk Executive. The corporate top down risk map is updated on an
annual basis and organizations&#146; risk owners are appointed to ensure the development of the <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">bottom-up</FONT> risk mapping, as well as the implementation and monitoring of risk mitigation action plans both at
corporate and organizations levels. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Reporting Obligations in IFRS </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We are incorporated in The Netherlands and our shares are listed on Euronext Paris and Borsa Italiana. Consequently, we
are subject to an EU regulation issued on September&nbsp;29, 2003 requiring us to report our results of operations and Consolidated Financial Statements using IFRS. As from January&nbsp;1, 2009 we are also required to prepare a <FONT
STYLE="white-space:nowrap">semi-annual</FONT> set of accounts using IFRS reporting standards. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We use U.S.
GAAP as our primary set of reporting standards, as U.S. GAAP has been our reporting standard since our creation in 1987. Until the SEC adopted rules allowing foreign private issuers to file financial statements prepared in accordance with IFRS
without reconciliation to U.S. GAAP, U.S. GAAP was the sole admitted reporting standard for companies like us whose shares are listed on the NYSE. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The obligation to report our Consolidated Financial Statements under IFRS requires us to prepare our results of operations using two&nbsp;different sets of reporting standards, U.S. GAAP and IFRS, which
are currently not consistent. Such dual reporting could materially increase the complexity of our investor communications. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Dividend Policy </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We formulated a revised dividend policy which was submitted for discussion at our Annual General Meeting of Shareholders held in Amsterdam on June&nbsp;21, 2013. As a result, we distribute a dividend on a
quarterly basis based on a semi-annual decision which is based on a proposal of our Managing Board and adopted by either our Supervisory Board or our meeting of shareholders proposed by our Supervisory Board. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our dividend policy reads as follows: we seek to use our available cash in order to develop and enhance our position in a
competitive semiconductor market while at the same time managing our cash resources to reward our shareholders for their investment and trust in us. Based on our results, projected capital requirements as well as business conditions and prospects,
our Managing Board proposes on a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">semi-annual</FONT> basis to our Supervisory Board, whenever deemed possible and desirable in line with our objectives and financial situation, the distribution of a quarterly cash
dividend, if any. Our Supervisory Board, upon the proposal of our Managing Board, decides or proposes to the meeting of shareholders on a <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">semi-annual</FONT> basis, in accordance with this
</FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">102
</FONT></P>

if any, of the quarterly cash dividend. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Based on our annual results, projected capital requirements as well

<TD WIDTH="8%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="justify"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><A NAME="tx622882_10"></A>Item&nbsp;9.</B></FONT></P></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Listing </B></FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Since 1994, our common shares have been traded on the NYSE under the symbol &#147;STM&#148; and CUSIP #861012102 and listed on the compartment A (large capitalizations) of Euronext Paris under the ISIN
Code NL0000226223. On June&nbsp;5, 1998, our common shares began trading on the Borsa Italiana. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On
September&nbsp;13, 2013, the NASDAQ OMX Group, Inc. announced the results of the annual <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">re-ranking</FONT> of the PHLX Semiconductor Sector Index (&#147;SOX&#148;). Effective September&nbsp;23, 2013, ST was removed
from the index. ST had been a member of the SOX since June&nbsp;23, 2003. The SOX is a widely followed, modified <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">capitalization-weighted</FONT> index composed of companies primarily involved in the design,
distribution, manufacture and sale of semiconductors in the U.S. markets. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On December&nbsp;5, 2013, the
Conseil Scientifique, an independent group of experts responsible for setting the rules and the periodical selection of the CAC 40 Index, announced the results of the annual <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">re-ranking</FONT> of the CAC 40 Index.
Effective December&nbsp;23, 2013, ST was removed from the CAC 40 and subsequently entered the CAC Next 20 Index. ST had been a member of the CAC 40 since November&nbsp;12, 1997. The CAC 40 Index is the main benchmark for Euronext Paris and contains
40 stocks selected among the top 100 market capitalization and the most active stocks listed on Euronext Paris. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Since March&nbsp;18, 2002, our common shares have been included in the FTSE MIB Index (formerly the S&amp;P/MIB and MIB
30 Index, respectively). The FTSE MIB Index measures the performance of 40 Italian equities and seeks to replicate the broad sector weights of the Italian stock market. The Index is derived from the universe of stocks trading on the Borsa Italiana
main equity market. Each stock is analyzed for size and liquidity, and the overall Index has appropriate sector representation. The FTSE MIB Index is market <FONT STYLE="white-space:nowrap">cap-weighted</FONT> after adjusting constituents for float.
Since January&nbsp;29, 2010, our common shares have been included in the FTSE MIB Dividend Index, the index which represents the cumulative value of ordinary gross dividends paid by the individual constituents of the underlying FTSE MIB Index,
expressed in terms of index points. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">103
</FONT></P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our common shares could be removed from the CAC Next 20 and the FTSE MIB
Indices at any time, and the exclusion or the announcement thereof could cause the market price of our common shares to drop significantly. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The following table sets forth, for the periods indicated, the high and low closing prices of our common shares on the NYSE, on the Euronext Paris and the Borsa Italiana. </FONT></P>

<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Of the 890,606,763 common shares outstanding as of December&nbsp;31, 2013, 41,678,079, or 4.7%, were registered in the
common share registry maintained on our behalf in New York and 598,223,930, or 67.2%, of our common shares outstanding were listed on Euroclear France and traded on Euronext Paris and on the Borsa Italiana in Milan. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="justify"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Market Information </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%" ALIGN="justify"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Since 1994, our shares have been traded on the NYSE. In addition, our shares have been listed on the Borsa Italiana since 1998 and on the Euronext Paris since 2001. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:18px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>
